domain: Kalshi API Authentication (RSA-PSS signing, API keys, WebSocket)
summary: Kalshi signs private REST and WebSocket requests with RSA-PSS via three KALSHI-ACCESS headers. All five critical claims in the original findings were independently confirmed against the primary docs.kalshi.com pages, including the millisecond timestamp, no-separator signed string, RSA-PSS/SHA256/DIGEST_LENGTH/base64 details, the host matrix, and the two API-key creation endpoints. The only correction is to an open question: the upload-public-key endpoint IS hard-restricted to Premier/Market Maker (403 otherwise).

endpoints.name: Generate API Key
endpoints.purpose: Kalshi generates the RSA keypair server-side and returns the private key once
endpoints.key_params: body name (required string), scopes (optional array, defaults to full access); returns api_key_id and private_key (RSA private key PEM)
endpoints.notes: Self-serve; private key cannot be retrieved again after the 201 response. Same flow as the UI 'Create New API Key' button (RSA_PRIVATE_KEY format). No tier restriction documented.

endpoints.name: Create API Key
endpoints.purpose: Upload your own RSA public key so Kalshi verifies your signatures
endpoints.key_params: body name (string), public_key (RSA public key PEM), scopes (optional); returns only api_key_id (no private key)
endpoints.notes: Restricted: 'allows users with Premier or Market Maker API usage levels to create API keys'; returns 403 'Forbidden - insufficient API usage level' for ineligible accounts. (GET to list / DELETE by id to revoke follow the standard REST pattern but were not separately re-fetched.)

endpoints.name: WebSocket connect
endpoints.purpose: Open an authenticated WS session
endpoints.key_params: three KALSHI-ACCESS headers at handshake; sign timestamp + 'GET' + '/trade-api/ws/v2'
endpoints.notes: Signs /trade-api/ws/v2 (with ws), NOT /trade-api/v2. Same three headers as REST.

concepts: API Key = Key ID (public, sent as KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY) plus an RSA private key (secret); Kalshi stores only the public key; signing is per-request with no session token.
Two key models: server-generated (POST /api_keys/generate returns the private key once) vs upload-your-own-public-key (POST /api_keys, Premier/Market Maker only).
Public market-data endpoints (series, events, markets, orderbook) require NO authentication headers; only portfolio/order/private endpoints need signing.
gotchas: TIMESTAMP is MILLISECONDS not seconds; the signed message has NO separators between timestamp, method, and path (e.g. 1703123456789GET/trade-api/v2/portfolio/balance).
Sign the path WITH the /trade-api/v2 prefix and WITHOUT the query string; salt_length is padding.PSS.DIGEST_LENGTH (32 bytes for SHA256), not MAX_LENGTH; the signature is base64-encoded, not hex.
WebSocket signs /trade-api/ws/v2 (with 'ws'), not /trade-api/v2; the server-generated private key is shown ONCE; demo hosts use the .kalshi.co TLD while production uses .kalshi.com.
The host/domain does not change the signature payload: legacy and new hosts all sign the same /trade-api/... path.
Uploading your own public key (POST /api_keys) is gated to Premier/Market Maker usage levels (403 otherwise); the self-serve generate endpoint has no documented tier gate.
open_questions: Allowed clock skew / timestamp validity window is not documented; keep clocks NTP-synced.
PEM variant is unstated beyond 'RSA_PRIVATE_KEY' / 'RSA private key in PEM format' (suggests PKCS1 but PKCS1 vs PKCS8 not confirmed); required RSA key size is not stated.
Per-tier rate limits are not quantified in the auth docs; legacy email/password + session-token flow is deprecated and not on current docs.
GET-list and DELETE-by-id behavior for /api_keys was inferred from the standard REST pattern, not separately re-fetched in this verification pass.
verification_notes: Claim 1 (signed string = ms-timestamp + METHOD + path, no separator, includes /trade-api/v2, excludes query; example 1703123456789GET/trade-api/v2/portfolio/balance): CONFIRMED verbatim at https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/quick_start_authenticated_requests — 'Sign the request path without query parameters', full path from API root, example matches exactly.

Claim 2 (RSA-PSS, SHA-256, MGF1 SHA-256, salt_length = PSS.DIGEST_LENGTH = 32 bytes, base64 signature): CONFIRMED at https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/quick_start_authenticated_requests — code shows padding.PSS(mgf=padding.MGF1(hashes.SHA256()), salt_length=padding.PSS.DIGEST_LENGTH) and 'Convert the signature to base64 string'. DIGEST_LENGTH for SHA256 = 32 bytes is correct.

Claim 3 (timestamp in Unix MILLISECONDS; headers KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY / KALSHI-ACCESS-SIGNATURE / KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP; WS uses same three headers, method GET, path /trade-api/ws/v2): CONFIRMED — 'Current time in milliseconds' on the authenticated-requests page; WS page shows the three headers (KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP: unix_timestamp_in_milliseconds) and signs timestamp + 'GET' + '/trade-api/ws/v2' (https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/quick_start_websockets).

Claim 4 (Prod REST external-api.kalshi.com, Demo REST external-api.demo.kalshi.co, Prod WS external-api-ws.kalshi.com, Demo WS external-api-ws.demo.kalshi.co, legacy api.elections.kalshi.com and demo-api.kalshi.co supported): CONFIRMED at https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/api_environments.md — all four new hosts match exactly, legacy hosts api.elections.kalshi.com (prod) and demo-api.kalshi.co (demo) listed as still supported, and 'the host does not change the signature payload'. Note the .co vs .com TLD distinction is real (demo = .kalshi.co, prod = .kalshi.com).

Claim 5 (POST /trade-api/v2/api_keys/generate returns api_key_id + private_key PEM once; POST /trade-api/v2/api_keys uploads public_key, Premier/Market Maker, returns only api_key_id; public market data needs NO auth): CONFIRMED — generate-api-key.md returns 201 with api_key_id + private_key (RSA PEM, 'cannot be retrieved again'); create-api-key.md takes public_key PEM, returns only api_key_id, and explicitly states 'allows users with Premier or Market Maker API usage levels' with 403 for insufficient level; quick_start_market_data states 'No authentication headers are required for the endpoints in this guide.' This also resolves the original open question — the upload endpoint IS hard-restricted to Premier/Market Maker.

domain: Kalshi Trade API — Environments, Base URLs & Connectivity
summary: Kalshi runs two isolated environments: Production (real money) and Demo/sandbox (fake funds). The recommended, dedicated REST base URLs are https://external-api.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2 (production) and https://external-api.demo.kalshi.co/trade-api/v2 (demo). Older shared hosts — https://api.elections.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2 (production) and https://demo-api.kalshi.co/trade-api/v2 (demo) — remain supported "for compatibility with existing clients," but the docs designate the external-api hosts as the "recommended hosts for API traders." WebSocket uses dedicated hosts: wss://external-api-ws.kalshi.com/trade-api/ws/v2 (prod) and wss://external-api-ws.demo.kalshi.co/trade-api/ws/v2 (demo), with the elections/demo-api hosts as the alternative (wss://api.elections.kalshi.com/trade-api/ws/v2 and wss://demo-api.kalshi.co/trade-api/ws/v2). The path prefix /trade-api/v2 (REST) or /trade-api/ws/v2 (WS) is constant across all hosts. Credentials are environment-specific: per the docs, "demo API keys only work against demo endpoints and production API keys only work against production endpoints." Authenticated requests use three headers — KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY, KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP (current time in MILLISECONDS), KALSHI-ACCESS-SIGNATURE — where the signature is an RSA-PSS/SHA256 signature over the concatenation timestamp + HTTP_METHOD + path; only the URL path (from the API root, query parameters stripped, no hostname) is used as the path component. Demo accounts are created at https://demo.kalshi.co/sign-up using mock personal info and sandbox test payment methods; API keys yield an RSA private key (.key) and a UUID API Key ID.

endpoints.name: Production REST (recommended/canonical)
endpoints.purpose: Canonical production Trade API base URL; provides access to ALL Kalshi markets despite the historical 'elections' naming on the legacy host
endpoints.key_params: Host external-api.kalshi.com; path prefix /trade-api/v2
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED on api_environments and quick_start_market_data pages as the 'recommended host for API traders' / production servers. Auth headers: KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY, KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP (ms), KALSHI-ACCESS-SIGNATURE (RSA-PSS SHA256, base64). Example: GET /trade-api/v2/series/KXHIGHNY.

endpoints.name: Production REST (legacy/alternative)
endpoints.purpose: Legacy/shared production base URL, still functional
endpoints.key_params: Host api.elections.kalshi.com; path prefix /trade-api/v2
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED. Listed as the 'Alternative' production host, kept 'supported for compatibility with existing clients.' NOT marked formally deprecated; external-api host is now recommended. Same path prefix /trade-api/v2.

endpoints.name: Demo REST (recommended/canonical)
endpoints.purpose: Canonical demo/sandbox Trade API base URL (fake funds)
endpoints.key_params: Host external-api.demo.kalshi.co; path prefix /trade-api/v2
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED on api_environments and demo_env pages. Note .co TLD (not .com). Requires demo-specific API keys; credentials not shared with production. Used in the authenticated-requests signing example.

endpoints.name: Demo REST (legacy/alternative)
endpoints.purpose: Legacy/alternative demo base URL, still functional
endpoints.key_params: Host demo-api.kalshi.co; path prefix /trade-api/v2
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED as the 'Alternative' demo host; external-api.demo host now recommended.

endpoints.name: Production WebSocket (recommended)
endpoints.purpose: Canonical production WebSocket base URL
endpoints.key_params: Host external-api-ws.kalshi.com; path /trade-api/ws/v2
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED. Dedicated WS host (note the -ws suffix). Alternative: wss://api.elections.kalshi.com/trade-api/ws/v2

endpoints.name: Demo WebSocket (recommended)
endpoints.purpose: Canonical demo WebSocket base URL
endpoints.key_params: Host external-api-ws.demo.kalshi.co; path /trade-api/ws/v2
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED. Dedicated demo WS host. Alternative: wss://demo-api.kalshi.co/trade-api/ws/v2

endpoints.name: Demo sign-up / website
endpoints.purpose: Create a demo account to obtain demo API keys and fake funds
endpoints.key_params: Mock name/address/SSN; real accessible email + memorable password
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED via Help Center article 13823775. Demo trading site is https://demo.kalshi.co/ (.co); the .com version is explicitly NOT a valid Kalshi domain. Test deposits: Visa Debit 4000 0566 5566 5556, Mastercard Debit 5200 8282 8282 8210, ACH user_good/pass_good (phone 415-555-0010, OTP 123456). Do NOT send real crypto to testnet addresses (unrecoverable). Production trading site is kalshi.com.

endpoints.name: API key generation (both envs)
endpoints.purpose: Generate/manage API credentials for the logged-in environment
endpoints.notes: Log in to the desired env (demo or prod), create key → download RSA Private Key (.key) + receive API Key ID (UUID, e.g. a952bcbe-ec3b-4b5b-b8f9-11dae589608c). Keys are environment-scoped. (Menu label per UI; exact label not re-quoted on fetched pages — see open questions.)
endpoints.key_params: Private Key (RSA .key file), API Key ID (UUID)

concepts: Two isolated environments: Production (real money) vs Demo/sandbox (fake funds)
Dedicated external-api hosts (recommended) vs legacy shared hosts (elections/demo-api), the latter 'supported for compatibility with existing clients'
Constant path prefix /trade-api/v2 (REST) and /trade-api/ws/v2 (WebSocket) across all hosts
Environment-scoped credentials: demo keys only work on demo, prod keys only on prod
RSA key-pair auth: API Key ID (UUID) + downloaded private .key file
Request signing uses RSA-PSS/SHA256 over the string timestamp + HTTP_METHOD + path; the path is the only URL component used (from the API root, query string stripped, no hostname)
Auth headers: KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY, KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP (milliseconds), KALSHI-ACCESS-SIGNATURE (base64)
Demo account creation with mock PII and sandbox test payment methods
WebSocket connectivity on dedicated -ws hosts
gotchas: Demo uses .co TLD (kalshi.co), production uses .com (kalshi.com) — easy to mix up. demo.kalshi.com is explicitly NOT a valid Kalshi domain (stated in Help Center article 13823775).
The production host name api.elections.kalshi.com sounds election-specific but serves ALL Kalshi markets — naming is historical.

There are TWO valid base URLs per environment; external-api* hosts are the recommended ones, while elections/demo-api hosts remain 'supported for compatibility with existing clients' (no stated deprecation/sunset date).
Production WebSocket host adds a -ws segment: external-api-ws.kalshi.com (REST host is external-api.kalshi.com without -ws). Same for demo: external-api-ws.demo.kalshi.co.
KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP is in MILLISECONDS, not seconds (docs explicitly warn 'Ensure timestamp is in milliseconds (not seconds)').

The signed message is the concatenation timestamp + HTTP_METHOD + path (e.g. 1703123456789GET/trade-api/v2/portfolio/balance). Use the full URL path from the API root with query parameters stripped — exclude the hostname and any query string. Example: for .../trade-api/v2/portfolio/orders?limit=5 you sign /trade-api/v2/portfolio/orders.
Demo and production credentials/funds are completely separate; you cannot reuse production API keys against the demo endpoint or vice versa.
The older trading-api.kalshi.com host is not listed in current docs — appears retired in favor of external-api/elections hosts (UNVERIFIABLE — absence of mention, not an explicit retirement notice).
Demo test deposits use sandbox test cards/ACH (e.g. Visa 4000 0566 5566 5556); never send real crypto to demo testnet addresses — such funds are unrecoverable.
open_questions: Are the legacy hosts (api.elections.kalshi.com, demo-api.kalshi.co) formally deprecated with a sunset date, or indefinitely supported? Docs only say 'supported for compatibility with existing clients' — no deprecation timeline given.
Is the old trading-api.kalshi.com host fully retired? It is referenced in older third-party guides/SDKs but does not appear in current docs.kalshi.com pages (absence of mention only).

The exact UI menu label for key generation ('Account & security → API Keys') and the downloaded-file/UUID details were not re-quoted verbatim on the fetched docs/help pages; verified conceptually but not word-for-word.

Do Perps APIs use the same external-api hosts or separate base URLs? The welcome page lists Predictions and Perps product lines (REST/WS/FIX) with spec files openapi.yaml / asyncapi.yaml / perps_openapi.yaml / perps_asyncapi.yaml, but the api_environments page covers only Predictions REST + WebSocket.
What are the FIX 4.4 protocol host(s)/ports for production and demo? Not documented on the api_environments page.
Exact wording/placement of any rate-limit tiers per environment was not found on the environments page.
verification_notes: Claim 1 (prod REST recommended external-api.kalshi.com, legacy api.elections.kalshi.com): CONFIRMED — api_environments lists 'Production (Recommended): https://external-api.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2' and 'Production (Alternative): https://api.elections.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2'; also confirmed by quick_start_market_data ('access market data directly from our production servers at https://external-api.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2'). Source: https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/api_environments

Claim 2 (demo REST recommended external-api.demo.kalshi.co, legacy demo-api.kalshi.co): CONFIRMED — api_environments lists 'Demo (Recommended): https://external-api.demo.kalshi.co/trade-api/v2' and 'Demo (Alternative): https://demo-api.kalshi.co/trade-api/v2'; demo_env page corroborates external-api.demo.kalshi.co. Source: https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/api_environments, https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/demo_env

Claim 3 (prod WS wss://external-api-ws.kalshi.com/trade-api/ws/v2, demo WS wss://external-api-ws.demo.kalshi.co/trade-api/ws/v2, legacy on elections/demo-api): CONFIRMED — api_environments lists recommended WS hosts plus alternatives wss://api.elections.kalshi.com/trade-api/ws/v2 and wss://demo-api.kalshi.co/trade-api/ws/v2. Source: https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/api_environments

Claim 4 (path prefix /trade-api/v2 REST, /trade-api/ws/v2 WS, identical across hosts): CONFIRMED — api_environments explicitly gives REST prefix /trade-api/v2 and WS prefix /trade-api/ws/v2; all listed base URLs share these prefixes. Source: https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/api_environments

Claim 5 (demo TLD .co, prod .com, demo.kalshi.com explicitly NOT valid): CONFIRMED, with source correction — the explicit 'the .com version is not a valid Kalshi domain' statement is in Help Center article 13823775, NOT the docs.kalshi.com demo_env page (which states demo.kalshi.co but does not mention/refute .com). Source: https://help.kalshi.com/en/articles/13823775-creating-and-using-a-demo-account

Claim 6 (credentials environment-isolated): CONFIRMED — api_environments states 'Credentials are not shared between environments, so demo API keys only work against demo endpoints and production API keys only work against production endpoints.' Source: https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/api_environments

Claim 7 (KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP in milliseconds): CONFIRMED — quick_start_authenticated_requests states timestamp is 'Current time in milliseconds' and troubleshooting says 'Ensure timestamp is in milliseconds (not seconds).' Source: https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/quick_start_authenticated_requests

Claim 8 (signature over path only, no hostname/query): CONFIRMED with clarification — the signed string is the concatenation timestamp + HTTP_METHOD + path (example: 1703123456789GET/trade-api/v2/portfolio/balance), where the path is the only URL component used ('Sign the full URL path from the API root, without query parameters'; query string stripped, hostname excluded). The original claim's phrasing 'path portion only' is accurate as to the URL component but the full signed message also includes the timestamp and HTTP method; algorithm is RSA-PSS with SHA256. Source: https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/quick_start_authenticated_requests

summary: Kalshi's market-data REST API lives under base URL https://external-api.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2 (the quick-start market-data guide uses this host; alt prod host api.elections.kalshi.com and demo hosts external-api.demo.kalshi.co / demo-api.kalshi.co are referenced elsewhere but were NOT confirmed on the market-data quick-start page). The data hierarchy is Series -> Event -> Market; a Market is one binary (yes/no) outcome. AUTH MODEL (corrected): most market-data endpoints are PUBLIC with no auth (OpenAPI security: []) — markets, market, trades, events, series, candlesticks, exchange status/schedule/announcements; BUT BOTH orderbook endpoints (single /markets/{ticker}/orderbook AND batch /markets/orderbooks) require auth — their OpenAPI security blocks override the root security:[] and demand KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY / -SIGNATURE / -TIMESTAMP headers. The single orderbook is NOT public (the public quick-start guide simply does not cover it). CRITICAL MIGRATION: Kalshi deprecated integer-cents fields (yes_bid, yes_ask, last_price, volume, open_interest, liquidity) and the legacy orderbook integer arrays, replacing them with fixed-point STRING fields: prices as dollar strings (\"0.4200\" = $0.42, up to 6 decimals) in *_dollars fields and contract counts as fixed-point strings (\"10.00\", 2 decimals) in *_fp fields. The changelog (Mar 3, 2026 entry) announced removal of legacy integer fields on March 12, 2026; today (2026-06-13) the dollar-string format is canonical and the old 1-99 cents fields are gone (the get-market schema shows only fixed-point fields). Orderbooks return only bids for both sides (yes_dollars + no_dollars arrays of [price_dollars, count_fp]); a YES bid at X equals a NO ask at (1.00 - X), and arrays are sorted ASCENDING so the best bid is the LAST element. Candlesticks come in market/batch/event/historical variants with period_interval of 1, 60, or 1440 minutes and unix-second start_ts/end_ts. Pagination uses an opaque cursor string with per-endpoint limit defaults (markets/trades 100 max 1000; events 200 max 200).

endpoints.name: Get Exchange Status
endpoints.purpose: Whether the exchange and trading are currently active (health/trading-hours gate before placing requests).
endpoints.key_params: none
endpoints.notes: Public, no auth (OpenAPI security=[], confirmed). Response fields: exchange_active (bool, false if core exchange not taking any state changes / under maintenance), trading_active (bool, true only during trading hours / not paused), exchange_estimated_resume_time (ISO8601 datetime, nullable/optional, can be extended/not guaranteed).

endpoints.name: Get Exchange Schedule
endpoints.purpose: Standard weekly trading hours and maintenance windows.
endpoints.key_params: none
endpoints.notes: Response: schedule.standard_hours (array of WeeklySchedule; each has start_time/end_time ISO8601 and monday..sunday each an array of DailySchedule with open_time/close_time as 'HH:MM' 24h strings in Eastern Time). schedule.maintenance_windows (array with start_datetime/end_datetime ISO8601). Multiple sessions per day possible.

endpoints.name: Get Exchange Announcements
endpoints.purpose: Exchange-wide announcements (e.g. outages).
endpoints.key_params: none
endpoints.notes: Array of announcements each with type, message, delivery_time, status. (Not deeply fetched.)

endpoints.name: Get Series List
endpoints.purpose: List/filter series (templates for recurring events).
endpoints.key_params: category (string, optional), tags (string, optional), include_product_metadata (bool, default false), include_volume (bool, default false), min_updated_ts (int64 unix seconds, optional). Confirmed: ALL params optional (category is NOT required).
endpoints.notes: Response { series: [Series] }. Series fields: ticker, frequency, title, category, tags ([string]|null), settlement_sources ([{name,url}]|null), contract_url, contract_terms_url, fee_type (enum quadratic|quadratic_with_maker_fees|flat), fee_multiplier (number), additional_prohibitions ([string]), product_metadata (obj, optional), volume_fp (fixed-point count string, optional with include_volume), last_updated_ts (datetime, optional). Confirmed fee_type enum and presence of volume_fp/last_updated_ts.

endpoints.name: Get Series
endpoints.purpose: Single series detail.
endpoints.key_params: path series_ticker; query include_volume (bool default false)
endpoints.notes: Returns GetSeriesResponse { series: Series } with same fields as list item.

endpoints.name: Get Events
endpoints.purpose: List/filter events with optional nested markets.
endpoints.key_params: limit (int 1-200, default 200 — confirmed max 200), cursor (string), status (enum unopened|open|closed|settled — NO 'paused', confirmed), series_ticker (string), tickers (comma-sep), with_nested_markets (bool default false), with_milestones (bool default false), min_close_ts (int unix s), min_updated_ts (int unix s)
endpoints.notes: Response { events:[Event], milestones (when with_milestones), cursor }. Event fields: event_ticker, series_ticker, title, sub_title, category (deprecated), mutually_exclusive (bool), collateral_return_type (string), strike_date (datetime nullable), strike_period (string nullable), markets (array, only when with_nested_markets=true), available_on_brokers (bool), product_metadata (nullable), last_updated_ts, fee_type_override (nullable), fee_multiplier_override (nullable), exchange_index (int). Note: an event mixing open+settled markets matches multiple status filters — use with_nested_markets to see per-market status. Multivariate events use a separate /events/multivariate endpoint.

endpoints.name: Get Event
endpoints.purpose: Single event, optionally with nested markets.
endpoints.key_params: path event_ticker; query with_nested_markets (bool, fills the deprecated 'markets' field)
endpoints.notes: Same Event shape as list. (Confirmed via search; not deeply fetched.)

endpoints.name: Get Markets
endpoints.purpose: List/filter markets with pagination.
endpoints.key_params: limit (int 0-1000, default 100 — confirmed), cursor (string), event_ticker, series_ticker, tickers (comma-sep), status (enum unopened|open|paused|closed|settled — confirmed), min_close_ts/max_close_ts, min_created_ts/max_created_ts, min_updated_ts, min_settled_ts/max_settled_ts (all int UNIX SECONDS, int64 — confirmed), mve_filter (enum only|exclude)
endpoints.notes: Response { markets:[Market], cursor }. NOTE the filter status enum (unopened/open/paused/closed/settled) differs from the Market.status field enum (initialized/inactive/active/closed/determined/disputed/amended/finalized — both confirmed). See Market fields under Get Market.

endpoints.name: Get Market
endpoints.purpose: Single market full detail.
endpoints.key_params: path ticker (string)
endpoints.notes: Market fields (current fixed-point schema, all confirmed present): ticker, event_ticker, market_type (binary|scalar), yes_sub_title, no_sub_title, title/subtitle (deprecated), created_time, updated_time, open_time, close_time, expected_expiration_time (nullable), latest_expiration_time, settlement_timer_seconds (int), status (initialized|inactive|active|closed|determined|disputed|amended|finalized), notional_value_dollars, yes_bid_dollars, yes_ask_dollars, yes_bid_size_fp, yes_ask_size_fp, no_bid_dollars, no_ask_dollars, last_price_dollars, previous_yes_bid_dollars, previous_yes_ask_dollars, previous_price_dollars, volume_fp, volume_24h_fp, liquidity_dollars (deprecated, returns '0.0000'), open_interest_fp, result (yes|no|scalar|''), can_close_early (bool), fractional_trading_enabled (deprecated, always true), expiration_value (string), occurrence_datetime (nullable), settlement_value_dollars (nullable), settlement_ts (nullable), fee_waiver_expiration_time (nullable), early_close_condition (nullable), strike_type (greater|greater_or_equal|less|less_or_equal|between|functional|custom|structured), floor_strike (double nullable), cap_strike (double nullable), functional_strike (nullable), custom_strike (obj nullable), rules_primary, rules_secondary, price_level_structure, price_ranges ([{start,end,step} USD strings]), mve_collection_ticker (nullable), mve_selected_legs (nullable), is_provisional (bool), exchange_index (int default 0). NO legacy integer-cents fields remain. All *_dollars are USD strings up to 6 decimals; all *_fp are contract-count strings (2 decimals); created/updated/open/close/expiration times are RFC3339 strings.

endpoints.name: Get Market Orderbook
endpoints.purpose: Resting bid liquidity for one market.
endpoints.key_params: path ticker; query depth (int, default 0; 0 or negative = all levels, 1-100 = N levels)
endpoints.notes: Response { orderbook_fp: { yes_dollars:[PriceLevel], no_dollars:[PriceLevel] } }. Each PriceLevel is a 2-element STRING array [price_dollars, count_fp] e.g. ['0.1500','100.00']. ONLY BIDS returned for both sides — no asks. YES bid at X = NO ask at (1.00 - X). Best YES ask = 1.00 - highest NO bid; best NO ask = 1.00 - highest YES bid. Arrays sorted ASCENDING by price, so the highest/best bid is the LAST array element (confirmed on orderbook_responses guide). CORRECTION: this endpoint REQUIRES AUTH — its OpenAPI security block overrides the root security:[] and requires KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY / -SIGNATURE / -TIMESTAMP headers. It is NOT public (the public market-data quick-start guide does not include orderbook).

endpoints.name: Get Multiple Market Orderbooks
endpoints.purpose: Batch fetch orderbooks for up to 100 markets.
endpoints.key_params: tickers (required, string array, min 1 / max 100 items, max 200 chars each; form/explode=true so repeated query params)
endpoints.notes: Response { orderbooks: [ { ticker, orderbook_fp:{yes_dollars,no_dollars} } ] } — an ARRAY of objects with a ticker field, NOT a map keyed by ticker (confirmed). Requires auth headers (KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY / -SIGNATURE / -TIMESTAMP) — same as the single-orderbook endpoint; BOTH orderbook endpoints require auth.

endpoints.name: Get Trades
endpoints.purpose: Public trade tape (executed trades), optionally filtered by market.
endpoints.key_params: limit (int 0-1000, default 100 — confirmed), cursor (string), ticker (QUERY param, not path — confirmed), min_ts/max_ts (int UNIX SECONDS), is_block_trade (bool)
endpoints.notes: Response { trades:[Trade], cursor }. Trade fields (confirmed verbatim): trade_id, ticker, count_fp (count string 2 dec, e.g. '10.00'), yes_price_dollars (USD string up to 6 dec, e.g. '0.5600'), no_price_dollars, taker_outcome_side ('yes'|'no'), taker_book_side ('bid'|'ask'), created_time (RFC3339), is_block_trade (bool). Legacy taker_side deprecated — use taker_outcome_side / taker_book_side.

endpoints.name: Get Market Candlesticks
endpoints.purpose: OHLC price/bid/ask history for one live market.
endpoints.key_params: path series_ticker AND ticker (both required — confirmed); query start_ts (req, unix s, candles ending on/after), end_ts (req, unix s, candles ending on/before), period_interval (req, enum 1|60|1440 minutes — confirmed), include_latest_before_start (bool default false, prepends synthetic candle)
endpoints.notes: Response { ticker, candlesticks:[MarketCandlestick] }. MarketCandlestick top-level fields (confirmed): end_period_ts (int64 unix s, inclusive period end), yes_bid (object {open_dollars,low_dollars,high_dollars,close_dollars}), yes_ask (object same OHLC), price (object {open/low/high/close/mean/previous/min/max _dollars, all nullable when no trades}), volume_fp, open_interest_fp. All prices dollar strings (6 dec); counts fixed-point (2 dec).

endpoints.name: Batch Get Market Candlesticks
endpoints.purpose: Candlesticks for multiple markets in one call.
endpoints.key_params: market_tickers (req, comma-separated, max 100 — confirmed), start_ts (req unix s), end_ts (req unix s), period_interval (req minutes, schema min 1 / int32; 1/60/1440 are the documented set), include_latest_before_start (bool optional default false)
endpoints.notes: Response { markets:[ {market_ticker, candlesticks:[MarketCandlestick]} ] }. Same MarketCandlestick shape and units as single-market candlesticks. Path does NOT include series_ticker (unlike the per-market candlestick path) — confirmed.

endpoints.name: Get Event Candlesticks
endpoints.purpose: Aggregated candlesticks across all markets in an event.
endpoints.key_params: path series_ticker AND ticker(=event_ticker) — confirmed; query start_ts (req int64), end_ts (req int64), period_interval (req, 1|60|1440 minutes)
endpoints.notes: Response (confirmed): market_tickers ([string]), market_candlesticks (array of candlestick arrays, one per market), adjusted_end_ts (adjusted end if the requested result would exceed maxAggregateCandidates size limit). Per-candle fields same as MarketCandlestick (yes_bid/yes_ask/price in dollars, volume_fp/open_interest_fp counts).

endpoints.name: Get Historical Market Candlesticks
endpoints.purpose: Candlesticks for settled/archived markets removed from the live data set.
endpoints.key_params: path ticker (no series_ticker — confirmed); query start_ts (req unix s), end_ts (req unix s), period_interval (req enum 1|60|1440 minutes)
endpoints.notes: Same candlestick field shape/units as live endpoint, but for archived markets. Use this when the regular per-series candlestick endpoint no longer returns a settled market. Path uses just {ticker}, no series_ticker — confirmed.

concepts: Hierarchy Series -> Event -> Market: a Series is a recurring template (settlement sources, rules, fee type); an Event is one occurrence; a Market is one binary yes/no outcome within an event.
Binary yes/no market: buying YES at price p (in dollars, 0-1) pays $1 if outcome is YES; NO is the complement, priced (1 - p). A YES bid at X is mechanically identical to a NO ask at (1.00 - X).
Fixed-point dollars representation: prices are STRINGS like '0.4200' meaning $0.42 (up to 6 decimals); they replaced the old integer-cents (1-99) fields.
Fixed-point counts (_fp): contract quantities are STRINGS like '10.00' (2 decimals) to support fractional contracts; they replaced legacy integer count fields.
Orderbook = resting bids only on both yes and no sides; asks are implied by the opposite side. Arrays ascending => best bid is last element.

Auth model: market-data read endpoints are public EXCEPT the two orderbook endpoints (single and batch), which require KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY/-SIGNATURE/-TIMESTAMP auth headers per their OpenAPI security blocks.
Pagination via opaque cursor: pass the returned cursor on the next request; empty cursor means no more pages.
Market.status lifecycle (initialized/inactive/active/closed/determined/disputed/amended/finalized) is distinct from the list-filter status enum (unopened/open/paused/closed/settled).
Candlestick period_interval is fixed to 1, 60, or 1440 minutes (minute/hour/day).
Ticker conventions: series ticker like KXHIGHNY; event and market tickers extend the series (e.g. KXHIGHNY-<date>, with market adding a strike segment), though docs do not give a strict format spec.
Strike types (greater/greater_or_equal/less/less_or_equal/between/functional/custom/structured) plus floor_strike/cap_strike define how a numeric/scalar outcome maps to YES settlement.
gotchas: Prices are NO LONGER integer cents (1-99). They are dollar STRINGS ('0.4200'). The task's '1-99 cents' assumption is the legacy model; the changelog removed cent fields on March 12, 2026. Treat *_dollars strings as canonical and parse as decimals, not ints.

Field names changed: yes_bid -> yes_bid_dollars, last_price -> last_price_dollars, volume -> volume_fp, open_interest -> open_interest_fp, liquidity -> liquidity_dollars (deprecated, always '0.0000'). Do not expect bare yes_bid/volume.
Orderbook arrays are sorted ASCENDING, so the best (highest) bid is the LAST element, not the first. Easy to grab the wrong level.
Orderbook returns ONLY bids (yes_dollars + no_dollars). To get the best YES ask you must compute 1.00 - (highest NO bid), and best NO ask = 1.00 - (highest YES bid).

AUTH (corrected): BOTH orderbook endpoints require auth. The single-market Get Market Orderbook (/markets/{ticker}/orderbook) is NOT public despite the rest of market-data being public — its OpenAPI security block overrides the global security:[] and requires KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY/-SIGNATURE/-TIMESTAMP. Same for the batch /markets/orderbooks.

Two different 'status' vocabularies: the markets/events list filter uses unopened/open/paused/closed/settled (events has NO 'paused'), but the Market.status field uses initialized/inactive/active/closed/determined/disputed/amended/finalized. They are not interchangeable.

Timestamp units are mixed: query filter params (min_ts/max_ts, *_close_ts, start_ts, end_ts, *_created_ts) are integer UNIX SECONDS, but Market/Event/Trade object time fields (open_time, close_time, created_time, settlement_ts) are RFC3339 datetime STRINGS. candlestick end_period_ts is unix seconds (int64).
limit defaults/maxes differ per endpoint: markets & trades default 100 / max 1000; events default 200 / max 200 (min 1).

Per-market candlesticks require BOTH series_ticker and ticker in the path (/series/{series_ticker}/markets/{ticker}/candlesticks). The batch candlesticks (/markets/candlesticks) and historical (/historical/markets/{ticker}/candlesticks) do NOT take series_ticker. Easy to construct the wrong path.
Get Multiple Market Orderbooks returns an ARRAY of {ticker, orderbook_fp}, not a map keyed by ticker, and requires auth headers (same as the single orderbook).
candlestick yes_bid / yes_ask are OBJECTS (OHLC of *_dollars), and the price.* OHLC fields are NULLABLE for periods with no trades; only the yes_bid/yes_ask snapshots are always present.
liquidity_dollars and fractional_trading_enabled are deprecated placeholders ('0.0000' and true) — do not rely on them.
Trades: yes_price_dollars + no_price_dollars are both present; taker_outcome_side ('yes'/'no') and taker_book_side ('bid'/'ask') replace the deprecated taker_side.
Settled markets get archived: if a market candlestick query stops returning data, switch to the /historical/.../candlesticks endpoint.
Event candlesticks may shrink the requested window: adjusted_end_ts is returned when the result would exceed maxAggregateCandidates (the numeric cap is not documented).
open_questions: Exact canonical production host: the market-data quick-start uses external-api.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2; api.elections.kalshi.com and the demo hosts (external-api.demo.kalshi.co / demo-api.kalshi.co) were NOT confirmed on the primary market-data docs page — confirm which host is current/preferred and whether the elections host is being retired.

Whether the public quick-start guide's 'no auth required' statement is meant to exclude the orderbook endpoint explicitly (it lists public endpoints but the orderbook OpenAPI requires auth) — confirm the exact list of endpoints the guide covers.

Max number of candlesticks returnable per request: event candlesticks reference 'maxAggregateCandidates' via adjusted_end_ts but the numeric value is not stated; per-market/batch/historical do not document a cap.
Exact ticker format grammar for event and market tickers (segments/delimiters, strike encoding); quick-start example only showed the series ticker KXHIGHNY.

Default tick size / minimum price increment per market: price_level_structure and price_ranges (start/end/step) govern it, but the typical default (e.g. $0.01) and which markets allow sub-penny ($0.0001) ticks are not enumerated.
Rate limits for public market-data endpoints (tiers/requests-per-second) are not covered on the market-data quick-start page; likely on a separate rate-limits/access page.
Whether the demo orderbook endpoints share the same auth requirement as production.
verification_notes: CLAIM 1 (base URL): PARTIALLY CONFIRMED. https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/quick_start_market_data states the production base URL verbatim as https://external-api.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2 and mentions ONLY that host. The alternate prod host api.elections.kalshi.com and the demo hosts (external-api.demo.kalshi.co / demo-api.kalshi.co) were NOT present on this page — UNVERIFIABLE from the market-data quick-start; treat them as carried-over claims to confirm elsewhere.

CLAIM 2 (legacy integer fields removed ~2026-03-12; *_dollars / *_fp canonical): CONFIRMED. https://docs.kalshi.com/changelog Mar 3 2026 entry states legacy integer count fields (with _fp equivalents) and integer cents price fields (with _dollars equivalents) 'will be removed on March 12, 2026' (an earlier Jan entry listed yes_bid/yes_ask/no_bid/no_ask/last_price as deprecated). https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/market/get-market shows ONLY fixed-point string fields (FixedPointDollars / FixedPointCount) and NO legacy integer fields. Removal date is March 12, 2026 (precise).

CLAIM 3 (orderbook shape: orderbook_fp with yes_dollars/no_dollars, bids-only, 2-element STRING arrays, ascending sort, best bid = LAST): CONFIRMED. https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/orderbook_responses states 'Kalshi's orderbook only returns bids, not asks', arrays are 'sorted by price in ascending order' with the 'highest bid (best bid) is the last element', and 'A YES BID at price X is equivalent to a NO ASK at price ($1.00 - X)'. Levels are [price_dollars string, count_fp string]. Matches get-market-orderbook reference.

CLAIM 4 (single orderbook PUBLIC, multi orderbook AUTH): CORRECTED. BOTH orderbook endpoints require auth. https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/market/get-market-orderbook OpenAPI security block OVERRIDES the root security:[] and requires KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY / -SIGNATURE / -TIMESTAMP — the single-market orderbook is NOT public. https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/market/get-multiple-market-orderbooks likewise requires those three headers. The quick-start 'public, no auth' statement applies to the endpoints it covers (markets/trades/candlesticks/events/exchange status), which do NOT include the orderbook. (Contrast: https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/exchange/get-exchange-status shows security:[] = genuinely public, demonstrating the security blocks really do differ per endpoint.)

CLAIM 5 (Get Markets limit 0-1000 default 100; filter status unopened|open|paused|closed|settled; Market.status initialized|inactive|active|closed|determined|disputed|amended|finalized): CONFIRMED. https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/market/get-markets shows limit 0–1000 default 100, filter status enum exactly {unopened, open, paused, closed, settled}, and Market.status enum exactly {initialized, inactive, active, closed, determined, disputed, amended, finalized}. Timestamp filter params are int64 unix seconds.

CLAIM 6 (Get Events limit 1-200 default 200; status unopened|open|closed|settled, NO paused): CONFIRMED. https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/events/get-events shows limit min 1 / max 200 / default 200 and status enum exactly {unopened, open, closed, settled} with no 'paused'.

CLAIM 7 (period_interval = 1|60|1440 only; start_ts/end_ts unix seconds; max-candles cap): CONFIRMED for the enum and units on https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/market/get-market-candlesticks ('Valid values are 1, 60, or 1440', start_ts/end_ts int64 unix seconds). The batch endpoint schema lists period_interval as int32 min 1 (looser), but 1/60/1440 are the documented set. Max-candles cap: UNVERIFIABLE numerically — event candlesticks reference 'maxAggregateCandidates' via adjusted_end_ts but no number is given.

CLAIM 8 (per-market candlesticks path needs series_ticker; batch = /markets/candlesticks market_tickers max 100): CONFIRMED. https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/market/get-market-candlesticks = /series/{series_ticker}/markets/{ticker}/candlesticks (both required). https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/market/batch-get-market-candlesticks = /markets/candlesticks with market_tickers comma list 'maximum 100', no series_ticker. Event candlesticks = /series/{series_ticker}/events/{ticker}/candlesticks and historical = /historical/markets/{ticker}/candlesticks also confirmed.

CLAIM 9 (Get Trades = /markets/trades, ticker as QUERY param): CONFIRMED. https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/market/get-trades path is /markets/trades, method GET, ticker is a query parameter ('Filter by market ticker'), not a path segment. limit 0–1000 default 100; min_ts/max_ts unix seconds; Trade fields trade_id/ticker/count_fp/yes_price_dollars/no_price_dollars/taker_outcome_side/taker_book_side/created_time/is_block_trade confirmed verbatim.

CLAIM 10 (exchange_active vs trading_active distinct booleans; exchange_estimated_resume_time nullable datetime): CONFIRMED. https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/exchange/get-exchange-status shows exchange_active (bool, false if exchange not taking state changes / under maintenance), trading_active (bool, false outside exchange hours), and exchange_estimated_resume_time (date-time string, nullable/optional). Endpoint is public (security:[]).

domain: Kalshi Trading API — Orders (placement, amendment, cancellation, lifecycle)
summary: Kalshi exposes two coexisting order APIs under the same /trade-api/v2 base (host https://external-api.kalshi.com). The LEGACY ("V1") order API lives at /portfolio/orders* and models orders with side=yes|no + action=buy|sell, prices as integer cents (yes_price/no_price, 1-99) OR fixed-point dollar strings (yes_price_dollars/no_price_dollars, up to 6 decimals), and contract counts as integer (count) OR fixed-point strings (count_fp, 2-decimal granularity 0.01). There is NO 'type' field in the V1 request body — order type (limit vs market) is inferred: supplying a price makes it a limit order; omitting price and supplying buy_max_cost (cents) makes it a market/Fill-or-Kill buy. The NEW "V2" order API lives at /portfolio/events/orders* and uses a single-book model with side=bid|ask, count and price as required fixed-point strings (price in fixed-point dollars, e.g. "0.0100" = 1 cent), and requires time_in_force and self_trade_prevention_type. time_in_force enum is fill_or_kill | good_till_canceled | immediate_or_cancel. Order status enum (current) is resting | canceled | executed; the 'pending' value was announced for removal in the changelog dated Nov 20 2025 with expected release Nov 27 2025 (and is now gone). Idempotency is via client_order_id: resubmitting the same client_order_id is deduplicated/rejected. Lifecycle edits: amend (price/size, may forfeit queue priority), decrease (only quantity reduction allowed), cancel (zeroes the resting remainder, returns reduced_by_fp). Legacy endpoints are being deprecated (no earlier than May 21 2026) with escalating rate-limit costs (cost multiplier rising 10x->15x->50x->100x across May 25 / Jun 1 / Jun 4 2026); integer cent/count fields were removed from REST + WebSocket responses Mar 12 2026 in favor of *_fp and *_dollars.

endpoints.name: Create Order (V1 / legacy)
endpoints.purpose: Submit a single order using the legacy yes/no + buy/sell model.
endpoints.key_params: Required: ticker(string,min1), action(buy|sell), side(yes|no). Optional: client_order_id(string), count(int>=1, whole contracts) OR count_fp(string, 0-2 decimals, min granularity 0.01; if both given must match), yes_price/no_price(int 1-99 CENTS) OR yes_price_dollars/no_price_dollars(string fixed-point dollars, up to 6 decimals), time_in_force(fill_or_kill|good_till_canceled|immediate_or_cancel), expiration_ts(int64, Unix SECONDS), buy_max_cost(int CENTS — triggers Fill-or-Kill market buy behavior), post_only(bool), reduce_only(bool), sell_position_floor(int, only 0; DEPRECATED use reduce_only), self_trade_prevention_type(taker_at_cross|maker), order_group_id(string), cancel_order_on_pause(bool), subaccount(int 0-63, default 0), exchange_index(int, default 0, only 0 supported).
endpoints.notes: No 'type' field in request — limit vs market is inferred from presence of price vs buy_max_cost. Response wraps an 'order' object (order_id,user_id,client_order_id,ticker,action,side,outcome_side,book_side,type[limit|market],status[resting|canceled|executed],yes_price_dollars,no_price_dollars,fill_count_fp,remaining_count_fp,initial_count_fp,taker_fees_dollars,maker_fees_dollars,taker_fill_cost_dollars,maker_fill_cost_dollars,expiration_time,created_time,last_update_time,...). Auth via KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY/-SIGNATURE/-TIMESTAMP(ms). CORRECTED: the create-order doc page literally states 'Rate limit: 100 tokens per request' for this single-order endpoint (likely reflecting the legacy-deprecation cost escalation in effect by Jun 2026; the generic default is 10 and batch create bills 10 tokens/order). CONFIRMED on this page: 'Each user is limited to 200,000 open orders at a time.'

endpoints.name: Create Order (V2 / new events model)
endpoints.purpose: Submit a single order in the new single-book bid/ask + fixed-point model (recommended migration target).
endpoints.key_params: Required: ticker(string), side(bid|ask — YES-leg perspective; bid=buy YES, ask=sell YES), count(string FixedPointCount, 0-2 decimals, e.g. '10.00'), price(string FixedPointDollars, up to 6 decimals; e.g. '0.0100'=1 cent), time_in_force(fill_or_kill|good_till_canceled|immediate_or_cancel), self_trade_prevention_type(taker_at_cross|maker). Optional: client_order_id(string), expiration_time(int Unix SECONDS, for GTT orders), post_only(bool,default false), cancel_order_on_pause(bool,default false), reduce_only(bool,default false), subaccount(int>=0,default0), order_group_id(string), exchange_index(int,default0,only 0 supported).
endpoints.notes: Returns HTTP 201 with order_id(req), client_order_id(opt echo), fill_count(req, FixedPointCount), remaining_count(req, FixedPointCount), average_fill_price & average_fee_paid(FixedPointDollars, present when fill_count>0), ts_ms(int, epoch MILLISECONDS). Error codes 400/401/409(Conflict)/429(RateLimit)/500, each with code/message/details/service. side and price/count semantics differ from V1; time_in_force and self_trade_prevention_type are REQUIRED here (optional in V1). Demo host: https://external-api.demo.kalshi.co/trade-api/v2.

endpoints.name: Batch Create Orders (V1)
endpoints.purpose: Submit multiple orders in one request.
endpoints.key_params: Body: 'orders' array of CreateOrderRequest objects (same fields as Create Order V1: required ticker/side/action plus optional count,count_fp,yes_price,no_price,*_dollars,expiration_ts,time_in_force,buy_max_cost,post_only,reduce_only,self_trade_prevention_type,order_group_id,cancel_order_on_pause,subaccount,exchange_index).
endpoints.notes: Returns 201 with BatchCreateOrdersResponse.orders[] of {client_order_id?(nullable), order?(nullable), error?(nullable ErrorResponse)}. Rate cost: '10 tokens per order in the batch — billed per item, so total cost for a batch of N orders is N x 10'. Max batch size has no absolute number; docs say it 'scales with your tier's write budget — see Rate Limits and Tiers'. V2 equivalent: POST /trade-api/v2/portfolio/events/orders/batched.

endpoints.name: Batch Cancel Orders (V1)
endpoints.purpose: Cancel multiple orders in one request.
endpoints.key_params: Body: 'orders' array (current) of {order_id(string,required), subaccount(int, 0 primary/1-63, optional), exchange_index(int,default0)}; OR 'ids' array of strings (DEPRECATED).
endpoints.notes: Returns BatchCancelOrdersResponse.orders[] of {order_id(req), reduced_by_fp(string,req — contracts canceled), order?(nullable), error?(nullable)}. Rate cost: '2 tokens per order in the batch — billed per item, total for N cancels is N x 2'. Max batch 'scales with your tier's write budget'. V2 equivalent: DELETE /trade-api/v2/portfolio/events/orders/batched. NOTE: same /portfolio/orders/batched path as Batch Create but with DELETE instead of POST.

endpoints.name: Amend Order
endpoints.purpose: Change price and/or size of a resting order.
endpoints.key_params: Required: ticker(string), side(yes|no), action(buy|sell). Optional: subaccount(int 0-63,default0), client_order_id(string, original), updated_client_order_id(string, new post-amend), yes_price/no_price(int 1-99 CENTS), yes_price_dollars/no_price_dollars(FixedPointDollars up to 6 dp), count(int>=1) OR count_fp(FixedPointCount, must match count if both), exchange_index(int,default0). RULE: 'Exactly one of yes_price, no_price, yes_price_dollars, and no_price_dollars must be passed.'
endpoints.notes: Queue priority (verbatim): 'Amending a resting order preserves queue position only when the amendment decreases size. All other amendments — like increasing size or changing price forfeit queue position and place the order at the back of the queue.' Returns AmendOrderResponse {old_order, order}. Codes 200/400/401/404/500. V2 path: /trade-api/v2/portfolio/events/orders/{order_id}/amend.

endpoints.name: Decrease Order
endpoints.purpose: Reduce the contract quantity of an existing order (only quantity edit type that lowers count).
endpoints.key_params: Optional subaccount(int, 0 primary / 1-63, default0), exchange_index(int,default0). Conditional: 'Exactly one of reduce_by(/reduce_by_fp) or reduce_to(/reduce_to_fp) must be provided.' reduce_by(int,min1)=subtract that many; reduce_to(int,min0)=set remaining to that level; *_fp are fixed-point string variants (must match integer if both given).
endpoints.notes: Decrease-only (verbatim): 'This is the only kind of edit available on order quantity.' Also: 'Cancelling an order is equivalent to decreasing an order amount to zero.' Returns 200 DecreaseOrderResponse with full 'order' object (updated fill_count_fp/remaining_count_fp/initial_count_fp). reduce_by was added alongside reduce_to per the May 12 2026 changelog (documented on the V2 events path POST /trade-api/v2/portfolio/events/orders/{order_id}/decrease). UNVERIFIED: the prior '~100 tokens' rate cost for decrease was not found on the decrease page. V2 path: /trade-api/v2/portfolio/events/orders/{order_id}/decrease.

endpoints.name: Cancel Order
endpoints.purpose: Cancel a single resting order.
endpoints.key_params: Path: order_id(string,required). Query: subaccount(int, 0 primary/1-63, default0), exchange_index(int, default0).
endpoints.notes: Does NOT delete; it 'reduce[s] the order completely, essentially zeroing the remaining resting contracts on it' (partial fills retain fill history). Returns 200 {order(updated state), reduced_by_fp(string — contracts canceled)}. Rate cost 2 tokens per request. Codes 401/404/500. V2 path: DELETE /trade-api/v2/portfolio/events/orders/{order_id}.

endpoints.name: Get Order
endpoints.purpose: Retrieve a single order by ID.
endpoints.key_params: Path: order_id(string,required). No query params.
endpoints.notes: Returns GetOrderResponse{order}. Required fields: order_id,user_id,client_order_id,ticker,side(yes|no, DEPRECATED — to be removed before May 14 2026),action(buy|sell, DEPRECATED — removed before May 14 2026),outcome_side(yes|no),book_side(bid|ask),type(limit|market),status(resting|canceled|executed),yes_price_dollars,no_price_dollars(FixedPointDollars up to 6 dp),fill_count_fp,remaining_count_fp,initial_count_fp(FixedPointCount 2-dec),taker_fees_dollars,maker_fees_dollars,taker_fill_cost_dollars,maker_fill_cost_dollars. Optional/nullable: expiration_time,created_time,last_update_time(ISO date-time),self_trade_prevention_type(taker_at_cross|maker),order_group_id,cancel_order_on_pause(bool),subaccount_number,exchange_index. Rate cost 2 tokens.

endpoints.name: Get Orders (list)
endpoints.purpose: List/filter the user's orders with pagination.
endpoints.key_params: Query: ticker(string), event_ticker('comma-separated list, maximum 10'), min_ts(int Unix sec), max_ts(int Unix sec), status(resting|canceled|executed), limit(int, default 100, min 1, max 1000), cursor(string, from previous response), subaccount(int, 0 primary/1-63; if omitted defaults to ALL subaccounts).
endpoints.notes: Response { orders: [Order...], cursor: string }. Empty cursor means no further pages. Status enum is the same 3 values resting/canceled/executed (no 'open'/'pending'/'all').

concepts: Two coexisting order APIs sharing /trade-api/v2 base (host external-api.kalshi.com): legacy '/portfolio/orders*' (side yes/no + action buy/sell) and new '/portfolio/events/orders*' (single-book side bid/ask, fixed-point).

Order type (limit vs market) is NOT a request field in V1 — it is inferred: price present => limit; price absent + buy_max_cost => market/Fill-or-Kill buy. 'type' (limit|market) only appears in the Order RESPONSE object.

Dual price representation: integer cents (yes_price/no_price, 1-99) vs fixed-point dollar strings (yes_price_dollars/no_price_dollars, up to 6 decimals). V2 uses only fixed-point dollars via the 'price' field.
Dual count representation: integer count (whole contracts) vs fixed-point string count_fp (2-decimal granularity, minimum 0.01). Responses use *_fp fields after the Mar 12 2026 integer removal.

time_in_force values: fill_or_kill, good_till_canceled, immediate_or_cancel. expiration_ts (V1) / expiration_time (V2) give a GTC order a rest-until time, in Unix SECONDS (turning it into a good-till-time order).
Idempotency via client_order_id: generate a UUID per order; the API rejects duplicate submissions with the same client_order_id, enabling safe retries after network issues.
Order status lifecycle: resting -> executed and/or canceled. The 'pending' status was announced for removal in the changelog dated Nov 20 2025 with expected release Nov 27 2025 (now removed).
Queue priority economics in amend: only size-decreasing amendments keep queue position; price changes or size increases go to the back of the queue.
Cancel is implemented as a full decrease (zeroing remaining contracts), returning reduced_by_fp; decrease is the only quantity edit that can lower count, and a cancel equals decreasing to zero.
self_trade_prevention_type controls how a user's own crossing orders are handled (taker_at_cross | maker); required in V2, optional in V1.

Rate limiting is token-based (per-request cost; batch endpoints bill per item: create=10/order, cancel=2/order; single cancel & get-order=2). The legacy single Create Order page states 100 tokens/request (consistent with the in-progress legacy cost escalation). Max 200,000 open orders per user.
Subaccounts (0=primary, 1-63) and exchange_index (shard, currently only 0 supported, default 0) are threaded through nearly all order endpoints.
gotchas: V1 'yes_price'/'no_price' are INTEGER CENTS (1-99). The '_dollars' variants are STRINGS in fixed-point dollars. Do not mix units. In V2 the single 'price' field is fixed-point DOLLARS — '0.0100' means 1 cent ($0.01), NOT 100 cents. (The quick-start text loosely labels '0.0100' as '1 cent' but the literal value is dollars.)
expiration_ts / expiration_time are in Unix SECONDS, but the auth header KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP and the V2 response ts_ms are in MILLISECONDS. Different units in the same domain.
buy_max_cost is in CENTS and, when set, forces Fill-or-Kill behavior (used to express a market buy). It is not a separate 'market' type flag.
count_fp / FixedPointCount granularity is 0.01 (2 decimals); price fixed-point dollars allow up to 6 decimals. These precisions differ.
V1 and V2 use DIFFERENT side semantics: V1 side=yes|no with action=buy|sell; V2 side=bid|ask (bid=buy YES, ask=sell YES). Mapping is easy to get backwards.
Cancel does not delete the order record — it zeroes remaining contracts and returns the order plus reduced_by_fp; partial fills keep their fill history. The order then shows status canceled.
sell_position_floor is DEPRECATED (only value 0) — use reduce_only instead. The task's 'sell_position_capped' field was NOT found in current docs.
Batch create vs batch cancel use the SAME path /portfolio/orders/batched but different HTTP verbs (POST vs DELETE).
Amend requires ticker+side+action even though you reference an existing order_id, and exactly one of yes_price/no_price/yes_price_dollars/no_price_dollars must be supplied.
decrease requires exactly one of reduce_by(_fp) OR reduce_to(_fp); reduce_by subtracts, reduce_to sets the target remaining — easy to confuse.

Legacy /portfolio/orders* endpoints have escalating rate-limit costs (multiplier 10x->15x->50x->100x on May 25 / Jun 1 / Jun 4 2026) leading into deprecation (no earlier than May 21 2026); the legacy single Create Order page already shows 100 tokens/request. Prefer V2 going forward.
Order status filter on Get Orders accepts only resting|canceled|executed; there is no 'open'/'pending'/'all' value.
The 'pending' removal changelog entry is DATED Nov 20 2025 but its expected RELEASE was Nov 27 2025 — the changelog post date is not the effective date.
V1 Order response side/action fields are themselves marked deprecated (slated for removal before May 14 2026); use outcome_side (yes/no) and book_side (bid/ask) instead.
open_questions: What is the DEFAULT time_in_force when the field is omitted in V1? Docs do not state it (commonly good_till_canceled in practice, but unconfirmed in the official spec).
What is the absolute maximum number of orders per batch (create and cancel)? Docs only say it 'scales with your tier's write budget' without a hard number.
The task mentioned 'sell_position_capped' — this field was NOT found in current docs; only sell_position_floor (deprecated, value 0) exists.
Exact current set of self_trade_prevention_type values — docs consistently list taker_at_cross and maker only; whether additional values exist is unclear.

Exact current rate-limit token cost of a SINGLE V1 Create Order: the create-order page reads '100 tokens per request' while batch create bills 10 tokens/order and the generic default is 10 — the page figure may reflect the in-progress legacy cost escalation rather than a static base cost.
The decrease endpoint's per-request token cost is not stated on its page (the earlier '~100 tokens' figure is unverified).

Status of the announced (Nov 21 2025) breaking change: cannot specify both immediate_or_cancel and expiration_ts (past expirations rejected) — changelog lists release as TBD, so unclear if currently enforced.
Whether the V1 batch and single endpoints are still live as of 2026-06-13, given deprecation 'no earlier than May 21 2026' and the legacy cost escalation that completes Jun 4 2026.

domain: Kalshi Trading API — Portfolio & Account endpoints
summary: The portfolio domain covers a member's account state: balance/portfolio value, positions (split into market_positions and event_positions), fills (executed trades), settlements (resolved markets), orders (resting/historical), a single-order lookup, and a total-resting-order-value summary (FCM-only). All endpoints live under the base path /trade-api/v2/portfolio/* . Per the docs quick-start, the production base URL is https://external-api.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2 and demo is https://external-api.demo.kalshi.co/trade-api/v2 (the analyst's earlier api.elections.kalshi.com / demo-api.kalshi.co hosts are NOT what the current docs show). All requests require RSA-PSS (SHA256) authenticated headers KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY, KALSHI-ACCESS-SIGNATURE, KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP (timestamp in milliseconds); the signature is over timestamp+method+path (full /trade-api/v2/... path, no query string). The MOST load-bearing fact for this domain: Kalshi has migrated from legacy integer-cent / integer-count fields to fixed-point STRING fields ending in _dollars (e.g. yes_price_dollars, market_exposure_dollars) and _fp (e.g. count_fp, position_fp). Per the changelog, the legacy integer fields that have _fp/_dollars equivalents were REMOVED on 2026-03-12 — so as of today (2026-06-13) the positions/fills/orders schemas expose only the string variants for those values (confirmed: no legacy integer cent fields appear in the current positions/fills/orders schemas). Fields WITHOUT a fixed-point equivalent remain integer cents: balance/portfolio_value (Get Balance), revenue and value (settlements), and total_resting_order_value. Pagination is cursor-based: response returns a cursor string; pass it as ?cursor= on the next request; continue until cursor is null/empty. limit defaults to 100, documented range 1–1000 on the per-endpoint portfolio specs (the general pagination guide says limit is 'typically 1-100', a conflict noted below). A subaccount query param (0 = primary, 1–63) is supported across portfolio endpoints. Directional fields side/action are deprecated in favor of outcome_side (yes/no) and book_side (bid/ask); note a doc inconsistency on the removal date (see gotchas).

endpoints.name: Get Balance
endpoints.purpose: Get the member's available trading balance and total portfolio value.
endpoints.key_params: subaccount (integer, optional, 0=primary default, 1-63=subaccount).
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED against get-balance page. Response: balance (int64, CENTS, verbatim 'Member's available balance in cents. This represents the amount available for trading'); balance_dollars (string fixed-point dollars, verbatim 'Member's available balance as a fixed-point dollar string', up to 6 decimals e.g. '0.5600'; for direct members carries centi-cent $0.0001 precision per the 2026-05-28 changelog while integer balance truncates sub-cent); portfolio_value (int64, CENTS, verbatim 'Member's portfolio value in cents. This is the current value of all positions held' — i.e. market value of positions only, NOT balance+positions); updated_ts (int64 Unix timestamp; the get-balance page describes it as the last-update timestamp and the docs annotate timestamps in milliseconds); balance_breakdown (array, optional) with sub-fields exchange_index (int, default 0) and balance (string fixed-point dollars). Scope: read::portfolio_balance. Auth required.

endpoints.name: Get Positions
endpoints.purpose: Get the member's positions, split into market-level and event-level aggregates.
endpoints.key_params: cursor (string); limit (int, min 1, max 1000, default 100); count_filter (string, comma-separated; ONLY allowed values are 'position' and 'total_traded' — restricts to positions with non-zero values of those fields); ticker (string); event_ticker (string, single event only); subaccount (int, default 0). NOTE: there is NO settlement_status query param documented for this endpoint (CORRECTED — the analyst's settlement_status was not found in the docs).
endpoints.notes: Returns two arrays plus cursor. market_positions[]: ticker; position_fp (FixedPointCount string, 2 decimals, negative=NO positive=YES); market_exposure_dollars (FixedPointDollars); realized_pnl_dollars (FixedPointDollars); total_traded_dollars (FixedPointDollars); fees_paid_dollars (FixedPointDollars); resting_orders_count (int32, DEPRECATED); last_updated_ts (date-time). event_positions[]: event_ticker; event_exposure_dollars (FixedPointDollars); realized_pnl_dollars (FixedPointDollars); total_cost_dollars (FixedPointDollars); total_cost_shares_fp (FixedPointCount string, YES+NO combined); fees_paid_dollars (FixedPointDollars). CONFIRMED: no legacy integer-cent fields present; only _fp/_dollars strings. Exact spelling is resting_orders_count (plural).

endpoints.name: Get Fills
endpoints.purpose: Get the member's executed fills (trade executions).
endpoints.key_params: ticker (string); order_id (string); min_ts (int64 Unix timestamp, 'filter items after'); max_ts (int64 Unix timestamp, 'filter items before'); limit (int64, min 1, max 1000, default 100); cursor (string); subaccount (int, 0-63, omit for all subaccounts).
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED against get-fills page. Response: fills[] + cursor. Fill fields: fill_id (string); trade_id (string, legacy alias of fill_id); order_id; ticker; market_ticker (legacy alias of ticker); outcome_side (yes/no); book_side (bid/ask); count_fp (FixedPointCount string, 2 decimals); yes_price_dollars (FixedPointDollars string); no_price_dollars (FixedPointDollars string); fee_cost (string fixed-point dollars); is_taker (boolean, true=removed liquidity); created_time (date-time, optional ISO-8601); ts (int64 Unix timestamp, legacy, optional); subaccount_number (int, optional); side (yes/no, DEPRECATED); action (buy/sell, DEPRECATED). Legacy integer count/yes_price/no_price (cents) NOT present (removed 2026-03-12). NOTE: the get-fills page labels side/action removal date as 'May 14, 2026', which conflicts with the changelog's 'not removed before May 28, 2026'.

endpoints.name: Get Settlements
endpoints.purpose: Get the member's market settlements (resolved markets and payouts).
endpoints.key_params: limit (int, min 1, max 1000, default 100); cursor (string); ticker (string); event_ticker (string, single event); min_ts (int Unix timestamp, items after); max_ts (int Unix timestamp, items before); subaccount (int, 0-63).
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED against get-settlements page. Response: settlements[] + cursor. Settlement fields: ticker; event_ticker; market_result (enum yes|no|scalar); yes_count_fp (FixedPointCount string, YES contracts at settlement, 2 decimals); no_count_fp (FixedPointCount string, NO contracts); yes_total_cost_dollars (FixedPointDollars, cost basis); no_total_cost_dollars (FixedPointDollars); revenue (INTEGER CENTS, verbatim 'Total revenue earned from this settlement in cents (winning contracts pay out 100 cents each)' — no _dollars equivalent, retained); fee_cost (string fixed-point dollars); value (INTEGER CENTS, nullable, verbatim 'Payout of a single yes contract in cents'); settled_time (date-time, when market settled and payouts processed).

endpoints.name: Get Orders
endpoints.purpose: Get the member's orders (resting and historical).
endpoints.key_params: ticker (string); event_ticker (string, comma-separated list, max 10); min_ts (int64 Unix timestamp); max_ts (int64 Unix timestamp); status (string enum resting|canceled|executed); limit (int, min 1, max 1000, default 100); cursor (string); subaccount (int, 0-63, defaults to all). (Doc page is /api-reference/orders/get-orders.)
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED against orders/get-orders page. Response: orders[] + cursor. Order fields: order_id; user_id; client_order_id; ticker; outcome_side (yes/no); book_side (bid/ask); type (limit/market); status (resting/canceled/executed); yes_price_dollars (FixedPointDollars); no_price_dollars (FixedPointDollars); fill_count_fp (FixedPointCount); remaining_count_fp (FixedPointCount); initial_count_fp (FixedPointCount); taker_fill_cost_dollars (FixedPointDollars); maker_fill_cost_dollars (FixedPointDollars); taker_fees_dollars (FixedPointDollars); maker_fees_dollars (FixedPointDollars); created_time; last_update_time; expiration_time (nullable); order_group_id (nullable); self_trade_prevention_type (taker_at_cross|maker, nullable); cancel_order_on_pause (bool); subaccount_number (int, nullable); exchange_index (int, default 0); side (yes/no, DEPRECATED); action (buy/sell, DEPRECATED). No integer-cent variants in current schema. NOTE: get-orders page labels side/action removal as 'until May 14, 2026' (conflicts with changelog's May 28, 2026).

endpoints.name: Get Order
endpoints.purpose: Get a single order by its order_id.
endpoints.key_params: Path param order_id (string, required). (Documented under /api-reference/orders/get-order.)
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED: GET /trade-api/v2/portfolio/orders/{order_id}. Returns one Order object with the same fields as the Get Orders list element (order_id, user_id, client_order_id, ticker, outcome_side, book_side, type, status, yes_price_dollars, no_price_dollars, fill_count_fp, remaining_count_fp, initial_count_fp, taker_fill_cost_dollars, maker_fill_cost_dollars, taker_fees_dollars, maker_fees_dollars, created_time, last_update_time, expiration_time, self_trade_prevention_type, order_group_id, cancel_order_on_pause, subaccount_number, exchange_index, plus deprecated side/action). 404 if not found.

endpoints.name: Get Total Resting Order Value
endpoints.purpose: Get the total value of all resting orders (FCM members only — rare).
endpoints.key_params: No query parameters.
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED against get-total-resting-order-value page. Response: total_resting_order_value (INTEGER, CENTS, 'Total value of resting orders in cents'). Doc explicitly says 'This endpoint is only intended for use by FCM members (rare). Note: If you're uncertain about this endpoint, it likely does not apply to you.' NOTE: the doc page does NOT show an example value (the analyst's example {"total_resting_order_value": 50000} is illustrative, not from the doc). Auth required (RSA-PSS headers).

concepts: Two unit systems coexist: legacy integer values (count of contracts, prices/amounts in CENTS) vs new fixed-point STRING fields ending in _dollars (USD, up to 6 decimals) and _fp (contract counts, 2 decimals). Migration removed the legacy integer equivalents that had _fp/_dollars counterparts on 2026-03-12 (confirmed in changelog).
FixedPointDollars = string with up to 6 decimal places (e.g. '0.5600'); FixedPointCount = string with 2 decimals (e.g. '10.00').
Positions are reported at TWO granularities: market_positions (per market ticker) and event_positions (per event_ticker, aggregating YES+NO across the event).
position_fp sign convention: positive = YES contracts held, negative = NO contracts held.
outcome_side (yes/no) and book_side (bid/ask) are the current directional fields; legacy side/action (buy/sell) are deprecated.

Cursor pagination: response includes a cursor string; pass it as ?cursor=<value> for the next page; stop when cursor is null/empty (NOT when the data array is short). Per-endpoint specs say limit default 100 / max 1000; the general pagination guide says limit is 'typically 1-100'.
Subaccounts: subaccount query param, 0 = primary account (default), 1-63 = subaccounts; balance_breakdown (per exchange_index) exists for multi-exchange/subaccount breakdown.

Authentication: RSA-PSS (SHA256) signed requests with KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY, KALSHI-ACCESS-SIGNATURE, KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP (timestamp in milliseconds); signature is over timestamp+METHOD+full /trade-api/v2 path without query params.

Base host per current docs quick-start: production https://external-api.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2, demo https://external-api.demo.kalshi.co/trade-api/v2 (older/other Kalshi hosts like api.elections.kalshi.com or trading-api.kalshi.com are NOT what the current docs.kalshi.com quick-start shows).
Granular scopes exist, e.g. read::portfolio_balance for the balance endpoint.
portfolio_value (Get Balance) = market value of current positions only, per the verbatim doc text 'This is the current value of all positions held' (not balance + positions).
gotchas: CRITICAL UNIT TRAP: Within a single response some fields are CENTS integers and others are DOLLARS strings. balance, portfolio_value (Get Balance), revenue and value (settlements), and total_resting_order_value are INTEGER CENTS. Almost everything in positions/fills/orders is now a fixed-point DOLLAR or COUNT string (_dollars/_fp). Do not assume the whole payload shares one unit.

Legacy integer fields (position, market_exposure, realized_pnl, total_traded, count, yes_price, no_price, fee in cents) were REMOVED on 2026-03-12 (changelog) and are NOT in the current schema for positions/fills/orders (verified: get-positions, get-fills, orders/get-orders show only _fp/_dollars strings). Code relying on the old integer names will break; use position_fp, market_exposure_dollars, count_fp, yes_price_dollars, etc.

balance_dollars carries finer precision than integer balance for direct members (centi-cent $0.0001) as of the 2026-05-28 changelog entry; the integer balance truncates sub-cent amounts. Prefer balance_dollars for exactness.
Field spelling: it is resting_orders_count (plural 'orders'), an int32, on market_positions, and it is DEPRECATED. Total resting order value uses a separate FCM-only endpoint, not this field.

Get Positions has NO settlement_status query param in the docs, and count_filter accepts ONLY 'position' and 'total_traded' (comma-separated). Do not assume a settlement_status/all/settled/unsettled filter on positions.

Timestamps: min_ts/max_ts query params and the legacy ts/updated_ts response integers are Unix timestamps, but the docs do NOT explicitly state seconds vs milliseconds for the min_ts/max_ts filters (the auth header timestamp is explicitly milliseconds; treat filter unit as unverified). created_time/last_update_time/settled_time/last_updated_ts are ISO-8601 date-time strings.
event_ticker filter differs by endpoint: Get Positions and Get Settlements accept only a SINGLE event_ticker; Get Orders accepts a comma-separated list of up to 10.

Pagination limit conflict: per-endpoint portfolio specs say default 100 / max 1000, while the general pagination guide says 'typically 1-100' (implying max 100). The per-endpoint specs are the more specific source. Always stop paging when the returned cursor is null/empty, NOT when the data array is short.

side/action (buy/sell, yes/no) are present but DEPRECATED. DATE CONFLICT in the docs: the changelog says they 'will not be removed before May 28, 2026', while the get-fills and get-orders endpoint pages label the removal date as 'May 14, 2026'. Either way, do not build new logic on them — use outcome_side/book_side.

Get Total Resting Order Value is FCM-only (rare); a normal retail/API member generally cannot/should not rely on it — instead sum resting orders from Get Orders (status=resting). The doc page shows no example value.

Host/base URL: the current docs.kalshi.com quick-start uses production https://external-api.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2 and demo https://external-api.demo.kalshi.co/trade-api/v2. Do NOT assume api.elections.kalshi.com or demo-api.kalshi.co — they are not what the current quick-start documents. All portfolio paths carry the /trade-api/v2 prefix.

Doc location quirk: Get Orders and Get Order live under /api-reference/orders/ (not /api-reference/portfolio/), even though their runtime paths are /trade-api/v2/portfolio/orders[/{order_id}]. The /api-reference/portfolio/get-orders URL 404s.
open_questions: min_ts/max_ts unit on Get Fills/Get Orders/Get Settlements: docs label them 'Unix timestamp' without specifying seconds vs milliseconds. The auth header timestamp is explicitly milliseconds, but the filter unit is not documented — needs live verification (Kalshi historically used seconds for these filters).

Authoritative side/action removal date: the changelog says 'not removed before May 28, 2026' while the get-fills and get-orders endpoint pages say 'May 14, 2026'. Which is correct for the current live API (today is 2026-06-13, so under the May 14 reading they would already be gone, yet the endpoint pages still list them) needs live confirmation.

Canonical production host: the docs quick-start shows external-api.kalshi.com / external-api.demo.kalshi.co, but Kalshi has historically used trading-api.kalshi.com and api.elections.kalshi.com. Confirm against a live authenticated call which host currently serves /trade-api/v2/portfolio/*.
updated_ts (Get Balance) unit: page implies milliseconds but does not state it as plainly as the auth header — confirm seconds vs ms via a live response.
Full field list / sub-fields of any 'Get All Subaccount Balances' endpoint and whether balance_breakdown[].balance is dollars-string with 6 decimals — not deeply verified.
Whether any min_ts/max_ts or settlement_status-style filter exists on Get Settlements beyond ticker/event_ticker/min_ts/max_ts (none surfaced).
Whether the general pagination guide's 'typically 1-100' is an actual hard cap on some endpoints or just illustrative, given per-endpoint specs say max 1000.
verification_notes: Claim 1 (Get Balance path + fields): CONFIRMED. get-balance page shows GET /portfolio/balance (i.e. /trade-api/v2/portfolio/balance) with balance (int64 cents), balance_dollars (string), portfolio_value (int64 cents), updated_ts (int64), balance_breakdown[]. (https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/portfolio/get-balance)

Claim 2 (portfolio_value verbatim = positions only): CONFIRMED verbatim — 'Member's portfolio value in cents. This is the current value of all positions held.' It is positions market value only, not balance+positions. (https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/portfolio/get-balance)
