domain: Kalshi Trading API — WebSocket streaming (Trade API v2)
summary: Kalshi exposes an authenticated WebSocket at wss://external-api-ws.kalshi.com/trade-api/ws/v2 (demo: wss://external-api-ws.demo.kalshi.co/trade-api/ws/v2). These dedicated external hosts were added to docs/examples on May 7 2026; current docs reference only these hosts (the previously shared host wss://api.elections.kalshi.com/trade-api/ws/v2 is NOT confirmed as still WS-compatible in current docs). The connection is authenticated during the HTTP handshake using the same RSA-PSS(SHA256) scheme as REST: headers KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY, KALSHI-ACCESS-SIGNATURE, KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP (Unix MILLISECONDS), where the signed string is timestamp + "GET" + "/trade-api/ws/v2". After connecting, clients send JSON commands {id, cmd, params} where cmd is subscribe / unsubscribe / update_subscription; the server replies with confirmation/data messages enveloped as {id?, type, sid, seq?, msg}. Valid channels: orderbook_delta, ticker, trade, fill, market_positions, market_lifecycle_v2, multivariate_market_lifecycle, multivariate (DEPRECATED multivariate-lookups, type 'multivariate_lookup'), communications, order_group_updates, user_orders, cfbenchmarks_value. ticker_v2 was removed Feb 12 2026 (use 'ticker'); market_lifecycle_v2 keeps its _v2 suffix. As of a Jan–Feb 2026 migration, prices are dollar strings with *_dollars suffixes (legacy integer cent fields removed ~Mar 12 2026) and contract quantities are fixed-point strings with *_fp suffixes (2 decimals; WS *_fp added Jan 22 2026); data-channel timestamps moved to *_ts_ms (Unix ms) with legacy ts (seconds) deprecated, while LIFECYCLE channel timestamps remain Unix SECONDS. The orderbook channel sends an orderbook_snapshot first then incremental orderbook_delta updates carrying a per-subscription top-level seq for gap detection (recover via update_subscription action=get_snapshot or send_initial_snapshot); keep-alive is server-driven Ping frames (0x9) every 10s with body 'heartbeat' that clients answer with Pong (0xA).

endpoints.name: WebSocket connection (handshake)
endpoints.purpose: Open the single authenticated streaming connection used for all channels.
endpoints.key_params: Handshake headers: KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY (API key id), KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP (Unix MILLISECONDS), KALSHI-ACCESS-SIGNATURE (base64 RSA-PSS SHA256 of the string `timestamp + "GET" + "/trade-api/ws/v2"`, salt_length=DIGEST_LENGTH, MGF1-SHA256).
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED (quick_start_websockets.md). Same auth/signing as REST. One connection multiplexes many subscriptions via sid. Server pings every 10s (frame 0x9, body 'heartbeat'); client must reply Pong (0xA). The legacy shared host api.elections.kalshi.com/trade-api/ws/v2 is NOT mentioned in current docs — treat its WS support as unconfirmed.

endpoints.name: subscribe command
endpoints.purpose: Subscribe to one or more channels, optionally scoped to markets.
endpoints.key_params: params.channels: string[] (required) from {orderbook_delta, ticker, trade, fill, market_positions, market_lifecycle_v2, multivariate_market_lifecycle, multivariate, communications, order_group_updates, user_orders, cfbenchmarks_value}; market scoping is per-channel: most use params.market_ticker (string) OR market_tickers (string[]); cfbenchmarks_value uses params.index_ids (array, or ["all"]) instead. Optional sharding params shard_key/shard_factor (see error codes 19–22). id is integer ≥0, unique per session.
endpoints.notes: Server replies {id, type:"subscribed", msg:{channel, sid}}; then begins streaming. Market-wide channels (market_lifecycle_v2, multivariate_market_lifecycle, multivariate) do not accept market filters (receive all). market_id/market_ids not supported for orderbook_delta/market_positions; use market_ticker(s).

endpoints.name: unsubscribe command
endpoints.purpose: Cancel one or more active subscriptions by server-assigned sid.
endpoints.key_params: params.sids: integer[] (required, min 1 item).
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED (websocket-connection.md). Server replies {id, sid, seq, type:"unsubscribed"}.

endpoints.name: update_subscription command
endpoints.purpose: Add/remove markets on an existing subscription, or request a fresh snapshot without changing it.
endpoints.key_params: params.sid (single) or params.sids (array with exactly one id); params.action (required) in {add_markets, delete_markets, get_snapshot}; params.market_tickers (string[]). Optional params.send_initial_snapshot (bool) and params.use_yes_price (bool, orderbook only — report no-side updates in yes-leg pricing).
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED (websocket-connection.md). action=get_snapshot returns an orderbook_snapshot for the requested market_tickers without modifying the subscription (gap-recovery path). Success reply {id, sid, seq, type:"ok", msg:{market_tickers:[...]}}.

endpoints.name: error message (server→client)
endpoints.purpose: Signals a rejected command or fault.
endpoints.key_params: msg.code: integer error code 1–28; msg.msg: human text. id echoes the offending command id.
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED full table (websocket-connection.md): 1 unable to process, 2 params required, 3 channels required, 4 sids required, 5 unknown command, 6 already subscribed, 7 unknown subscription id, 8 unknown channel, 9 authentication required, 10 channel error, 11 invalid parameter, 12 exactly one sid required, 13 unsupported action, 14 market ticker required, 15 action required, 16 market not found, 17 internal error, 18 command timeout, 19–22 shard_factor/shard_key validation, 23 match ids required, 24 index ids required, 25 buffer overflow, 26 market limit exceeded, 27 too many requests, 28 markets not found.

endpoints.name: orderbook_delta channel (PUBLIC market data)
endpoints.purpose: Maintain a live per-market orderbook: full snapshot then incremental price-level deltas.
endpoints.key_params: snapshot.msg: yes_dollars_fp & no_dollars_fp = arrays of [price_dollars(string), count_fp(string, 2dp)] (omitted if a side is empty). delta.msg: market_ticker, market_id (UUID), price_dollars(string), delta_fp(string SIGNED, 2dp), side ('yes'|'no'), ts_ms (Unix ms); optional client_order_id and subaccount (present only when YOUR order caused the change); legacy ts (RFC3339) deprecated.
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED (orderbook-updates.md). Envelope carries top-level seq that increments per message per sid; check for gaps. Recover via update_subscription get_snapshot (or resubscribe / send_initial_snapshot). use_yes_price IS a real optional orderbook subscribe/update param (confirmed on websocket-connection.md) — earlier 'unverified' note retracted. This is a public market-data channel (llms.txt groups it under Market Data Channels), not a private/user channel.

endpoints.name: ticker channel
endpoints.purpose: Market price/volume/open-interest and L1 (best bid/ask) updates.
endpoints.key_params: msg: market_ticker, market_id, price_dollars(string), yes_bid_dollars, yes_ask_dollars (string dollars), volume_fp & open_interest_fp (string, 2dp), dollar_volume & dollar_open_interest (integer dollars), ts (Unix s, deprecated), time (RFC3339, deprecated), ts_ms (Unix ms). L1 size fields yes_bid_size_fp / yes_ask_size_fp / last_trade_size_fp.
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED (market-ticker.md). Channel and type are both 'ticker'; 'ticker_v2' was REMOVED Feb 12 2026 (changelog). No legacy integer cent fields (yes_bid/yes_ask/price) remain in the current schema.

endpoints.name: trade channel (public trades)
endpoints.purpose: Real-time public executed trades per market.
endpoints.key_params: msg: trade_id (UUID), market_ticker, yes_price_dollars(string), no_price_dollars(string), count_fp(string, 2dp), taker_side ('yes'|'no'), taker_outcome_side ('yes'|'no'), taker_book_side ('bid'|'ask'), ts (s, deprecated), ts_ms (Unix ms).
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED (public-trades.md). Public market data but still requires the authenticated WS handshake.

endpoints.name: fill channel (user fills)
endpoints.purpose: Private notifications of the authenticated user's order fills.
endpoints.key_params: msg: trade_id, order_id, market_ticker, is_taker(bool), side('yes'|'no'), yes_price_dollars(string, ONLY yes price — no no_price_dollars), count_fp(string 2dp), action('buy'|'sell'), post_position_fp(string 2dp), fee_cost, client_order_id, purchased_side, outcome_side, book_side, subaccount, ts(s,deprecated), ts_ms(Unix ms).
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED (user-fills.md). Private/auth-required. Confirmed there is NO no_price_dollars field; side and action are separate.

endpoints.name: market_positions channel
endpoints.purpose: Private updates to the user's position in a market on trades/settlements.
endpoints.key_params: msg: user_id, market_ticker, position_fp(string 2dp), position_cost_dollars(string), realized_pnl_dollars(string), fees_paid_dollars(string), position_fee_cost_dollars(string), volume_fp(string 2dp), subaccount(int, optional). market_id NOT supported as filter.
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED (market-positions.md). Message type value is singular 'market_position'. No ts/ts_ms fields in the schema. Filters by market_ticker/market_tickers only.

endpoints.name: market_lifecycle_v2 channel
endpoints.purpose: Market state changes and event creation/fee notifications.
endpoints.key_params: market lifecycle msg: market_ticker, event_type in {created, activated, deactivated, close_date_updated, determined, settled, price_level_structure_updated, metadata_updated}; conditional open_ts, close_ts, determination_ts, settled_ts (Unix SECONDS), result, settlement_value (fixed-point dollars string), is_deactivated(bool), price_level_structure in {linear_cent, deci_cent, tapered_deci_cent}. event_lifecycle msg: event_ticker, title, subtitle, collateral_return_type (MECNET|DIRECNET|''), series_ticker, strike_date(opt), strike_period(opt).
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED (market-and-event-lifecycle.md). Channel name retains _v2 (unlike ticker). Market filters not supported (receives all). Timestamps here are in SECONDS. metadata_updated event added May 5 2026 (changelog) — CORRECTED from the prior 'May 11 2026'. fractional_trading_updated is no longer in the enum (consistent with removal).

endpoints.name: multivariate_market_lifecycle channel
endpoints.purpose: Lifecycle (and event) notifications for multivariate/combo (MVE) events.
endpoints.key_params: multivariate_market_lifecycle msg mirrors market_lifecycle_v2 (market_ticker, event_type, open_ts/close_ts/determination_ts/settled_ts in SECONDS, result, settlement_value, is_deactivated, price_level_structure). event_lifecycle msg: event_ticker, title, subtitle, collateral_return_type in {MECNET, DIRECNET, ''}, series_ticker, strike_date(opt), strike_period(opt).
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED (multivariate-market-and-event-lifecycle.md). Added Mar 19 2026 (changelog). market_ticker filters not supported (receives all).

endpoints.name: multivariate channel (DEPRECATED multivariate-lookups)
endpoints.purpose: Legacy collection-lookup notifications for multivariate collections (predates RFQs).
endpoints.key_params: Global subscription — NO filtering params. msg: collection_ticker, event_ticker, market_ticker, and selected_markets[] (each with event_ticker, market_ticker, side).
endpoints.notes: RESOLVED open question (multivariate-lookups-deprecated.md): 'multivariate' is a real but DEPRECATED channel ('this channel predates RFQs and should not be used for new integrations'); message type is 'multivariate_lookup'. Distinct from multivariate_market_lifecycle.

endpoints.name: user_orders channel
endpoints.purpose: Private real-time order lifecycle via a status field.
endpoints.key_params: msg: order_id, client_order_id, ticker (NOTE: 'ticker', not market_ticker), side('yes'|'no'), status in {resting, canceled, executed}, yes_price_dollars(string, 4dp), initial_count_fp, fill_count_fp, remaining_count_fp (string 2dp), created_ts_ms (Unix ms), last_updated_ts_ms(opt), subaccount_number(opt int).
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED (user-orders.md). Single message type 'user_order' (no separate created/updated types); no order_type/action/no_price_dollars fields. Uses 'ticker' field name unlike other channels.

endpoints.name: communications channel
endpoints.purpose: Private RFQ and quote (request-for-quote) notifications.
endpoints.key_params: envelope {type, sid, msg} (NO seq); e.g. quote_executed msg: quote_id, rfq_id, client_order_id, order_id, market_ticker, executed_ts (RFC3339/date-time string).
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED (communications.md). Auth-required. Envelope has no seq field.

endpoints.name: order_group_updates channel
endpoints.purpose: Private real-time order-group lifecycle and contract-limit updates.
endpoints.key_params: envelope {type:'order_group_updates', sid, seq, msg}. msg: event_type in {created, triggered, reset, deleted, limit_updated}, order_group_id(string), contracts_limit_fp(string 2dp; present only for created/limit_updated), ts_ms(int64, matching-engine Unix ms).
endpoints.notes: RESOLVED open question (order-group-updates.md). Auth-required. Envelope DOES carry seq.

endpoints.name: cfbenchmarks_value channel
endpoints.purpose: CF Benchmarks index value feed (e.g. BRTI, ETHUSD_RTI).
endpoints.key_params: Subscribe with params.index_ids (array of CF Benchmarks index IDs, e.g. ["BRTI","ETHUSD_RTI"], or ["all"]); market_ticker/market_tickers/market_id/market_ids NOT supported (error code 24 'Index IDs required'). msg: index_id(string), received_at(Unix ms), data(raw CF Benchmarks JSON string), avg_60s_data(object: value, window_size, timestamps), last_60s_windowed_average_15min(object, optional; only at :00/:15/:30/:45).
endpoints.notes: RESOLVED open question (cfbenchmarks-value.md). Distinct subscription param (index_ids) from all other channels.

concepts: Single multiplexed WS connection: one authenticated socket carries many subscriptions, each identified by a server-assigned integer sid
Command/response model: client sends {id, cmd, params}; server confirms with {id, type:'subscribed'|'unsubscribed'|'ok'|'error', sid, seq?, msg}
Message envelope for data: {type, sid, seq?, msg} where type names the channel/event and msg holds the payload
Snapshot-then-delta: orderbook_delta channel emits orderbook_snapshot first, then incremental orderbook_delta updates that must be applied in order

Per-subscription sequence numbers (seq) at the envelope top level for gap detection; recover via update_subscription get_snapshot, send_initial_snapshot, or resubscribe. seq presence varies by channel (orderbook_delta and order_group_updates carry seq; the communications envelope does NOT)
Cents→dollars migration: all prices are now dollar-denominated strings with *_dollars suffix (legacy integer cent fields yes_bid/yes_ask/price removed ~Mar 12 2026)

Fixed-point quantities: contract counts/sizes/positions are strings with *_fp suffix carrying 2 decimal places (count_fp, volume_fp, delta_fp, position_fp, open_interest_fp); user_orders prices use 4 decimals (WS *_fp fields added Jan 22 2026)

Timestamp evolution: *_ts_ms (Unix milliseconds) is current for DATA channels; bare ts (seconds)/time (RFC3339) are deprecated — but LIFECYCLE channels (market_lifecycle_v2, multivariate_market_lifecycle) use SECONDS for open_ts/close_ts/determination_ts/settled_ts
RSA-PSS(SHA256) handshake auth shared with REST: sign timestamp(ms)+'GET'+'/trade-api/ws/v2', base64; KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY/SIGNATURE/TIMESTAMP headers

Public vs private channels: PUBLIC market-data channels (orderbook_delta, ticker, trade, market_lifecycle_v2, multivariate_market_lifecycle, multivariate, cfbenchmarks_value) ride the authenticated session but apply no extra per-channel authorization; PRIVATE channels (fill, market_positions, user_orders, communications, order_group_updates) carry user data and require auth (error code 9 'Authentication required'). Note: orderbook_delta is PUBLIC, not private
Server-driven WebSocket Ping/Pong keep-alive every 10 seconds (frame 0x9 body 'heartbeat', answer with 0xA)

Per-channel market scoping differs: most channels use market_ticker/market_tickers; cfbenchmarks_value uses index_ids; market_lifecycle/multivariate channels accept no filter; optional shard_key/shard_factor can shard a channel's markets across connections
gotchas: Prices are DOLLARS as strings (e.g. "0.480", "0.0800"), NOT integer cents anymore — legacy integer cent fields (yes_bid, yes_ask, price, last_price) were deprecated/removed ~Mar 12 2026
Contract quantities are fixed-point STRINGS with 2 decimals (e.g. "333.00", delta_fp "-54.00") under *_fp names — not integers; user_orders yes_price_dollars uses 4 decimals ("0.3500")
delta_fp is signed (negative removes contracts at that price level); apply against the matching side ('yes'|'no') and price_dollars level
Lifecycle channel timestamps (open_ts, close_ts, determination_ts, settled_ts) are in SECONDS, whereas data channels' ts_ms is in MILLISECONDS — do not assume one unit globally
KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP is Unix MILLISECONDS, and it is the value prepended in the signed string before 'GET' and '/trade-api/ws/v2'
The signed path for the WS signature is literally '/trade-api/ws/v2' (no query string) even though the wss URL host differs from the REST host
market_id / market_ids are NOT accepted on orderbook_delta and market_positions; cfbenchmarks_value uses index_ids (not market_ticker at all)
Channel name is 'ticker' (singular) — 'ticker_v2' was REMOVED Feb 12 2026; but the lifecycle channel keeps its '_v2' suffix as 'market_lifecycle_v2'
Message 'type' values differ from channel names: market_positions channel → type 'market_position'; user_orders channel → type 'user_order'; the 'multivariate' channel → type 'multivariate_lookup'
user_orders uses the field name 'ticker' (not 'market_ticker') and a single 'user_order' type with status in {resting, canceled, executed}
seq presence varies: orderbook_snapshot/orderbook_delta and order_group_updates carry top-level seq; the communications envelope does NOT; 'subscribed' confirmations do not include seq
Keep-alive Ping is server→client every 10s with body 'heartbeat' (frame 0x9); the client must respond with Pong (0xA) — there is no JSON heartbeat message
fill channel includes yes_price_dollars only (no no_price_dollars); side and action ('buy'/'sell') are separate fields
'multivariate' is a real channel but is DEPRECATED (multivariate-lookups, predates RFQs) — distinct from multivariate_market_lifecycle; do not use it for new integrations

New external hosts external-api-ws.kalshi.com / external-api-ws.demo.kalshi.co were added to docs May 7 2026; current docs reference ONLY these — older shared hosts (api.elections.kalshi.com) are not confirmed WS-compatible
cfbenchmarks_value subscription requires index_ids (e.g. ["BRTI"] or ["all"]); supplying market_ticker yields error code 24 'Index IDs required'
open_questions: Legacy WS host: is wss://api.elections.kalshi.com/trade-api/ws/v2 (and demo-api.kalshi.co) still functional, or hard-cut to the new external-api-ws hosts? Current docs/changelog only document the external-api-ws hosts.

Does seq increment on EVERY data channel (ticker, trade, fill, market_positions) or only some? Confirmed present on orderbook_delta and order_group_updates, confirmed ABSENT on communications; ticker/trade/fill/market_positions JSON examples did not show a top-level seq.
Does seq reset to a deterministic starting value on (re)subscribe and is the first snapshot seq fixed (examples showed snapshot seq=2)?
shard_key / shard_factor subscribe params (implied by error codes 19–22) — exact semantics and which channels support market sharding were not documented on the pages fetched.
Idle/inactivity timeout before the server force-closes a connection that fails to Pong is not stated (only the 10s ping interval).

Per-connection limits: max subscriptions, max markets per subscription (error code 26 exists), and command/message rate limits (error code 27) — exact numeric caps were not found in the fetched pages.

Exact subscribe confirmation envelope shape ({id, type:'subscribed', msg:{channel, sid}}) was inferred from REST-style conventions; the precise subscribed-reply JSON was not quoted verbatim on the fetched pages.
event_fee_update message payload fields on market_lifecycle_v2 were not extracted in detail.
A parallel 'margin-ws' set of WebSocket docs exists (docs.kalshi.com/margin-ws/...); its relationship/differences to the standard trade-api WS were not investigated.
verification_notes: Claim 1 (URLs): CONFIRMED for production wss://external-api-ws.kalshi.com/trade-api/ws/v2 and demo wss://external-api-ws.demo.kalshi.co/trade-api/ws/v2 (quick_start_websockets.md; changelog May 7 2026). The sub-claim that legacy wss://api.elections.kalshi.com/trade-api/ws/v2 'still works' is UNVERIFIABLE — current docs and changelog reference only the external-api-ws hosts; softened in summary/endpoint.

Claim 2 (auth): CONFIRMED — quick_start_websockets.md gives headers KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY/SIGNATURE/TIMESTAMP, timestamp in MILLISECONDS, and signed string exactly `timestamp + "GET" + "/trade-api/ws/v2"` (base64 RSA-PSS SHA256, same scheme as REST).

Claim 3 (subscribe shape): CONFIRMED — {id, cmd:'subscribe', params:{channels:[...], market_ticker|market_tickers}} (quick_start_websockets.md, websocket-connection.md). Caveat: cfbenchmarks_value scopes via index_ids, not market_ticker.

Claim 4 (full channel set): CONFIRMED — websocket-connection.md 'Available Channels' lists exactly orderbook_delta, ticker, trade, fill, market_positions, market_lifecycle_v2, multivariate_market_lifecycle, multivariate, communications, order_group_updates, user_orders, cfbenchmarks_value; each verified on its own .md page. 'multivariate' = the deprecated multivariate-lookups channel (type 'multivariate_lookup').

Claim 5 (ticker_v2 removed / ticker current / market_lifecycle_v2 keeps _v2): CONFIRMED — market-ticker.md channel & type are 'ticker'; changelog Feb 12 2026 explicitly removed ticker_v2; market-and-event-lifecycle.md channel is 'market_lifecycle_v2'.

Claim 6 (prices *_dollars strings; quantities *_fp 2dp; user_orders 4dp): CONFIRMED across orderbook-updates.md, market-ticker.md, public-trades.md, user-fills.md, market-positions.md, user-orders.md; changelog Jan 22 2026 added WS *_fp fields. user-orders.md confirms yes_price_dollars 4 decimals.

Claim 7 (orderbook snapshot/delta schema): CONFIRMED — orderbook-updates.md: snapshot has yes_dollars_fp/no_dollars_fp arrays of [price_dollars, count_fp]; delta has price_dollars, signed delta_fp, side('yes'|'no'), ts_ms, optional client_order_id/subaccount.

Claim 8 (seq per-sid + get_snapshot recovery): CONFIRMED — orderbook-updates.md places seq at envelope top level for snapshot/delta consistency; websocket-connection.md confirms update_subscription action=get_snapshot. ADDITION: send_initial_snapshot and use_yes_price are also real optional params (websocket-connection.md), resolving the prior get_snapshot-vs-send_initial_snapshot open question — both exist.

Claim 9 (keep-alive): CONFIRMED verbatim — connection-keep-alive.md: 'Kalshi sends Ping frames (0x9) every 10 seconds with body heartbeat'; clients respond with Pong frames (0xA); clients may also send Ping. No JSON heartbeat.

domain: Kalshi FIX API (Financial Information eXchange protocol for order entry, market data, drop copy, RFQ, and post-trade)
summary: Kalshi offers a FIX API for institutional/high-throughput traders, documented at docs.kalshi.com/fix. It is FIXT.1.1 transport with application version FIX50SP2 (DefaultApplVerID tag 1137 = "FIX50SP2<9>") — NOT FIX 4.4 as some third-party blogs claim. Access is default for Premier-tier members or above; lower tiers must email institutional@kalshi.com. Authentication uses an RSA key pair: the public key is registered to obtain an API Key ID (UUID) used as SenderCompID, and Logon (35=A) carries a base64 RSA-PSS/SHA256 signature in RawData (96) over a SOH-joined pre-hash string of SendingTime+MsgType+MsgSeqNum+SenderCompID+TargetCompID. Connectivity is over TLS 1.2+ (plain TCP prohibited) to dedicated hosts/ports per session type (KalshiNR 8228, KalshiRT 8230, KalshiDC 8229, KalshiPT 8231, KalshiRFQ 8232 on mm.fix.elections.kalshi.com; KalshiMD 8233 on marketdata.fix.elections.kalshi.com); only one connection per API key. Order entry supports NewOrderSingle/Cancel/CancelReplace/MassCancel with limit-only orders (OrdType=2), prices in integer cents 1–99 by default or dollar/subpenny format (up to 4 decimals) when Logon tag 21005 UseDollars=Y. The docs cover message retransmission (KalshiRT/PT only, MessageRetentionPeriod tag 20200 up to 72h, default 24h), listener (read-only) sessions, drop copy (request-response history query), market data, market settlement, and RFQ. The docs do NOT give explicit "use FIX vs REST/WebSocket" latency guidance — FIX is positioned for institutional/HFT. All critical claims in the prior findings were re-verified against primary docs and confirmed (with a few minor extras corrected, e.g. the unverified "HeartBtInt default 30s").

endpoints.name: Order Entry session — No Retransmission (KalshiNR)
endpoints.purpose: Primary low-latency order entry session; does NOT support ResendRequest/retransmission — must set ResetSeqNumFlag=Y (141) on Logon
endpoints.key_params: TargetCompID=KalshiNR; SenderCompID=API Key UUID; Logon 35=A with RawData(96) RSA-PSS sig, HeartBtInt(108)>3s, EncryptMethod(98)=None<0>, DefaultApplVerID(1137)=FIX50SP2
endpoints.notes: Sessions IDed by TargetCompID+SenderCompID. One connection per API key. Can be run in listener (read-only) mode via Logon tag 20126 ListenerSession=Y (also requires tag 21011 SkipPendingExecReports=Y).

endpoints.name: Order Entry session — With Retransmission (KalshiRT)
endpoints.purpose: Order entry with message recovery; supports ResendRequest (35=2) and sequence-number persistence
endpoints.key_params: TargetCompID=KalshiRT; MessageRetentionPeriod tag 20200 up to 72 hours, default 24h (KalshiRT/PT only); ResendRequest lookback 24h default, up to 72h with extended retention
endpoints.notes: Retransmission supported only on KalshiRT and KalshiPT. KalshiRT retains message continuity across the maintenance window. Connection terminates if MsgSeqNum lower than expected; higher gaps recoverable via ResendRequest.

endpoints.name: Drop Copy session (KalshiDC)
endpoints.purpose: Request-response tool to query/recover historical execution reports (NOT a live feed)
endpoints.key_params: TargetCompID=KalshiDC; EventResendRequest (35=U1) with BeginExecID/optional EndExecID, EventResendComplete (35=U2) with ResendEventCount, EventResendReject (35=U3)
endpoints.notes: Explicitly 'not a traditional drop copy' — request-response pattern. Lookback limited to 3 hours; returns only ExecutionReport (35=8); rejects and pending orders excluded; resent messages get new seq numbers (reconcile via ExecID). Distinct from a listener session (live read-only shadow feed on KalshiRT).

endpoints.name: Post Trade session (KalshiPT)
endpoints.purpose: Post-trade reporting incl. MarketSettlementReport for market outcomes/position resolution
endpoints.key_params: TargetCompID=KalshiPT; supports retransmission and MessageRetentionPeriod tag 20200 up to 72h (default 24h)
endpoints.notes: One of two session types (with KalshiRT) supporting message retransmission/ResendRequest.

endpoints.name: RFQ session (KalshiRFQ)
endpoints.purpose: Request-for-Quote workflow for RFQ creators and market makers
endpoints.key_params: TargetCompID=KalshiRFQ; QuoteRequest (R), Quote (S), and related RFQ lifecycle/ack/status messages
endpoints.notes: Full RFQ lifecycle message set documented at /fix/rfq-messages.

endpoints.name: Market Data session (KalshiMD)
endpoints.purpose: Order book snapshots and incremental updates via FIX
endpoints.key_params: TargetCompID=KalshiMD; MarketDataRequest (V), MarketDataSnapshotFullRefresh (W), MarketDataIncrementalRefresh (X), SecurityStatus messages
endpoints.notes: Separate market data host (marketdata.fix.*) from order entry host (mm.fix.*), both prod and demo.

endpoints.name: Logon (35=A)
endpoints.purpose: Authenticate and establish the FIX session
endpoints.key_params: 98 EncryptMethod=None<0>; 96 RawData=base64 RSA-PSS(SHA256) signature; 108 HeartBtInt (integer >3 seconds); 1137 DefaultApplVerID=FIX50SP2<9>; 141 ResetSeqNumFlag=Y (required on non-retransmission sessions); optional 21005 UseDollars=Y, 20126 ListenerSession=Y (+21011 SkipPendingExecReports=Y), 20200 MessageRetentionPeriod (max 72h, KalshiRT/PT), 8013 CancelOrdersOnDisconnect (default N)
endpoints.notes: Pre-hash string = SendingTime + SOH + MsgType + SOH + MsgSeqNum + SOH + SenderCompID + SOH + TargetCompID. SendingTime in the pre-hash must exactly match tag 52 and be within 30s of server time (else SessionRejectReason 373=10), format YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS.mmm UTC. Logon is rate-limited.

endpoints.name: NewOrderSingle (35=D)
endpoints.purpose: Submit a new order
endpoints.key_params: 11 ClOrdID (UUID, max 64 chars); 38 OrderQty (decimal, fractional ok); 40 OrdType=2 Limit ONLY; 44 Price (integer cents 1–99, or dollars if UseDollars); 54 Side (1=Buy,2=Sell); 55 Symbol (market ticker); opt 18 ExecInst=6 PostOnly; 59 TimeInForce (0=Day,1=GTC,3=IOC,4=FOK,6=GTD); 126 ExpireTime (required for GTD); 2964 SelfTradePreventionType (1=Taker At Cross default,2=Maker); 21006 CancelOrderOnPause; 21009 MaxExecutionCost (dollars); 79 AllocAccount (subaccount 0–63); 526 SecondaryClOrdID (order group identifier); 448/452/453 Party fields (FCM)
endpoints.notes: Only limit orders. Past GTD dates treated as IOC. Idempotency via ClOrdID.

endpoints.name: OrderCancelRequest (35=F) / OrderCancelReplaceRequest (35=G)
endpoints.purpose: Cancel or modify (qty/price) an existing order
endpoints.key_params: Cancel required: 11 ClOrdID, 41 OrigClOrdID, 54 Side, 55 Symbol (optional 37 OrderID, Party fields, 79 AllocAccount). Replace required: 11 ClOrdID, 38 OrderQty, 40 OrdType, 41 OrigClOrdID, 54 Side, 55 Symbol; modifies OrderQty (increasing forfeits queue position) and Price
endpoints.notes: CancelReplace modifies OrderQty and Price without a full cancel.

endpoints.name: OrderMassCancelRequest (35=q)
endpoints.purpose: Cancel all resting orders for the session
endpoints.key_params: 11 ClOrdID; 530 MassCancelRequestType=6 (Cancel for session); response OrderMassCancelReport (35=r)
endpoints.notes: Rate-limited to 1 request/second (per connectivity docs).

endpoints.name: ExecutionReport (35=8)
endpoints.purpose: Order/fill lifecycle notifications
endpoints.key_params: 150 ExecType (0=New,F=Trade,4=Canceled,5=Replaced,8=Rejected,C=Expired,A=Pending New,6=Pending Cancel,E=Pending Replace); 39 OrdStatus; 14 CumQty; 151 LeavesQty; 31 LastPx; 32 LastQty
endpoints.notes: Listener sessions receive only ExecutionReports (read-only live shadow feed); Drop Copy returns only ExecutionReports on request.

endpoints.name: OrderGroupRequest (35=UOG) / OrderGroupResponse (35=UOH)
endpoints.purpose: Auto position management: cancel all orders in a group when a contract limit is hit
endpoints.key_params: 20131 OrderGroupAction (1=Create,2=Reset,3=Delete,4=Trigger,5=Update); 20130 OrderGroupID; 20132 OrderGroupContractsLimit (1–1,000,000); 79 AllocAccount (0–63); rolling 15-second window for limit eval
endpoints.notes: Order groups scoped per subaccount (AllocAccount must match for all ops). An update whose new limit would immediately trigger (per the 15s window) cancels all orders in the group; Trigger cancels immediately.

concepts: FIXT.1.1 transport with FIX50SP2 application layer (DefaultApplVerID tag 1137 = FIX50SP2<9>)
SenderCompID = API Key UUID (you); receiving SenderCompID = literal 'Kalshi'; TargetCompID = session name (KalshiNR/RT/DC/PT/RFQ/MD)
RSA key pair: register public key to obtain API Key ID; sign Logon with base64 RSA-PSS + SHA256
SOH-delimited pre-hash string (SendingTime,MsgType,MsgSeqNum,SenderCompID,TargetCompID) signed for Logon authentication
Session types map to distinct hosts and ports; order entry on mm.fix.* and market data on marketdata.fix.*; only one connection per API key
Retransmission vs non-retransmission sessions (KalshiRT/PT support ResendRequest and MessageRetentionPeriod tag 20200; KalshiNR and others require ResetSeqNumFlag=Y)
Listener sessions (real-time read-only ExecutionReport shadow feed via tag 20126 + 21011) vs Drop Copy (request-response history query, 3h lookback)
Cents-integer pricing (1–99) by default vs dollar/subpenny format (UseDollars tag 21005=Y, up to 4 decimals) affecting tags 6,31,44,132,133
Token-bucket rate limiting shared with REST; Logon and MassCancel (1/sec) specifically constrained
Order groups for automatic position-limit-based cancellation (35=UOG/UOH, tag 20131 OrderGroupAction)
Self-trade prevention (SelfTradePreventionType tag 2964: 1=Taker At Cross default, 2=Maker)
Subaccounts via AllocAccount (0–63) and FCM sub-accounts via Party fields (448/452/453)
A parallel FIX Margin API exists at /fix-margin/* for margin accounts
gotchas: Protocol is FIXT.1.1 / FIX50SP2 — third-party sources claiming 'FIX 4.4' are WRONG per official docs (DefaultApplVerID tag 1137 = FIX50SP2<9>).

Default Price (44) is INTEGER CENTS in range 1–99 (e.g. 72 = 72 cents), NOT dollars. Dollars/subpenny only when Logon tag 21005 UseDollars=Y, then up to 4 decimal places (e.g. 1.23c=0.0123, 72.5c=0.725, 99c=0.99); affects tags 6 AvgPx, 31 LastPx, 44 Price, 132 BidPx, 133 OfferPx.
Only OrdType=2 (Limit) is supported — there are NO market orders.

SendingTime (52) must EXACTLY match the value used in the pre-hash string AND be within 30 seconds of server time, format YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS.mmm in UTC; otherwise rejected with SessionRejectReason (373)=10.
Pre-hash string order is specific: SendingTime, MsgType, MsgSeqNum, SenderCompID, TargetCompID joined by SOH — wrong order = signature failure.
ResetSeqNumFlag=Y (141) is REQUIRED on all non-retransmission sessions (incl. KalshiNR) or the Logon is rejected; retransmission/ResendRequest only works on KalshiRT and KalshiPT.
Only ONE FIX connection allowed per API key; separate API keys are needed for concurrent connections.
HeartBtInt (108) must be an integer greater than 3 seconds; the session is dropped on a missed heartbeat. (The prior 'default 30s' value is NOT stated in the docs.)
Mass Cancel (35=q) is throttled to 1 request/second; other FIX app messages share the same token model as the equivalent REST endpoint; Logon is also rate-limited.
Plain TCP is prohibited; TLS 1.2+ mandatory.
Order groups are scoped per subaccount — operations fail unless the same AllocAccount (0–63) is passed; assign an order to a group via tag 526 SecondaryClOrdID.
TimeInForce GTD (6) requires ExpireTime (126); past GTD dates are treated as IOC.

Production order-entry host (mm.fix.elections.kalshi.com) differs from market-data host (marketdata.fix.elections.kalshi.com); demo uses fix.demo.kalshi.co and marketdata.fix.demo.kalshi.co. Demo ports match prod (8228–8233).
CheckSum (10) is a 3-char zero-padded modulo-256 value; BodyLength (9) counts from the tag after BodyLength up to (not including) the CheckSum field.
Two distinctly-spelled cancel tags exist: 8013 CancelOrdersOnDisconnect (Logon, default N) vs 21006 CancelOrderOnPause (per-order in NewOrderSingle) — do not confuse them.
Drop Copy (KalshiDC) is request-response with a 3-hour lookback and returns only ExecutionReports — it is NOT a live feed; use a listener session for real-time.
open_questions: The docs do NOT explicitly compare FIX vs REST vs WebSocket or give numeric latency/throughput guidance; positioning is implicitly 'institutional/high-frequency'. No published latency SLAs or colocation/cross-connect options found (AWS PrivateLink available on request via institutional@kalshi.com).

Maintenance window: connectivity.md CONFIRMS sessions may be disconnected during the maintenance window, that Kalshi does NOT initiate sequence-number resets during maintenance (clients should reset on reconnect), and that KalshiRT retains message continuity across it. The exact schedule (e.g. Thursday 03:00–05:00 ET) lives at /getting_started/maintenance_and_pauses, which was not fetched this pass.

CancelOnDisconnect tag is now resolved: tag 8013 'CancelOrdersOnDisconnect' (plural Orders, Logon, default N) on the authentication page; the separate per-order tag 21006 'CancelOrderOnPause' (singular Order) in NewOrderSingle is a different field.
No specific TLS cipher suite list, IP allowlist, or static IP addresses are published in the fetched pages.
Demo ports are now confirmed to mirror production for all six session types (8228 NR, 8229 DC, 8230 RT, 8231 PT, 8232 RFQ on fix.demo.kalshi.co; 8233 MD on marketdata.fix.demo.kalshi.co).

A parallel FIX Margin API exists (/fix-margin/* — authentication, connectivity, drop-copy, error-handling, listener-sessions, market-data, order-entry, order-groups) for margin accounts; its hosts/ports and differences from the standard FIX API were not mapped this pass.
Exact valid range/precision/minimum-tick rules for subpenny prices, and whether all markets support subpenny, are not fully specified beyond 'up to 4 decimal places'.

Whether OrderMassCancelRequest is restricted to specific session types is unclear: one auto-extraction of /fix/order-entry suggested 'KalshiNR only', but this was not corroborated and may be a parsing artifact — needs primary-doc confirmation.
