domain: Kalshi Trading API — Rate Limits & Access Tiers
summary: Kalshi rate-limits use a token-bucket model with five named API usage tiers: Basic (default on signup), Advanced (self-service upgrade), and Premier/Paragon/Prime (earned by trailing-30-day volume share or assigned manually). Each account has separate Read and Write buckets measured in tokens-per-second: Basic 200 read / 100 write; Advanced 300/300; Premier 1,000/1,000; Paragon 2,000/2,000; Prime 4,000/4,000. Buckets refill continuously at the per-second budget; Read buckets and Basic Write buckets hold 1 second of budget, while Write buckets above Basic (Advanced and above) hold up to 2 seconds (allowing a one-time burst of up to 2x the per-second budget). Most requests cost the default 10 tokens; non-default costs (e.g. Get Order = 2 tokens, Batch Cancel Orders = 2 tokens/order) are listed authoritatively at GET /trade-api/v2/account/endpoint_costs (whose response includes default_cost for context). Batch requests are billed per item (25 creates = 25 x 10 = 250 tokens) and the entire batch must fit in the bucket at arrival or it is rejected. A 429 returns {"error":"too many requests"} with no Retry-After / X-RateLimit-* headers and no penalty/cooldown — the bucket simply keeps refilling, and the docs explicitly advise to 'Apply exponential backoff on 429.' Live limits and tier are readable via GET /trade-api/v2/account/limits (with a separate perps counterpart GET /trade-api/v2/account/limits/perps); self-upgrade to Advanced is POST /trade-api/v2/account/api_usage_level/upgrade. NOTE: nearly every claim in the prior analyst pass was independently confirmed against primary docs; corrections are minimal (mostly additive — perps limits endpoint and a V2 batch-create variant).

endpoints.name: Get Account API Limits
endpoints.purpose: Return the authenticated account's current API usage tier and its read/write token-bucket configuration plus active volume grants.
endpoints.key_params: No request params. Auth headers required: KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY, KALSHI-ACCESS-SIGNATURE, KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP.
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED against the reference page. Returns GetAccountApiLimitsResponse: usage_tier (string), read (BucketLimit), write (BucketLimit), grants (array of ApiUsageLevelGrant). BucketLimit = { refill_rate (int, tokens/sec), bucket_capacity (int, max tokens) }. ApiUsageLevelGrant = { exchange_instance (ExchangeInstance enum: event_contract | margined), level (string e.g. premier/paragon/prime), expires_ts (int, nullable, Unix ts in SECONDS), source (string volume|manual) }. Documented error codes: 401, 500.

endpoints.name: Get Account API Limits (Perps / Margined)
endpoints.purpose: Return the read/write token-bucket configuration for the margined (perps) exchange lane, distinct from the Predictions (event_contract) lane.
endpoints.key_params: No request params. Same auth headers as /account/limits.
endpoints.notes: NEWLY CONFIRMED: the rate_limits page lists GET /account/limits/perps as a separate limits endpoint for the perps lane. No dedicated api-reference page was found (the /api-reference/account/get-account-api-limits-perps URL 404s), so the exact perps response schema and whether perps budget numbers match the Predictions schedule are not yet published — see open_questions.

endpoints.name: Upgrade Account API Usage Level
endpoints.purpose: Self-service grant of a permanent Advanced API usage-level grant (Basic -> Advanced).
endpoints.key_params: No request body documented. Requires that at least 1 of the user's last 100 Predictions orders was created via API.
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED against the reference page. 201 on success (Advanced grant created/refreshed); 401 Unauthorized; 403 when no API-created order is found among the latest 100 Predictions orders; 429 (this endpoint itself costs 30 tokens and draws from the Predictions Write bucket); 500. Currently only the Predictions ('event_contract') exchange instance is supported. Cannot self-upgrade beyond Advanced — Premier/Paragon/Prime are volume-earned or manually assigned.

endpoints.name: Get Endpoint Costs
endpoints.purpose: Authoritative public list of endpoints whose token cost differs from the default, so clients can compute sustained rate = budget / cost.
endpoints.key_params: None documented.
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED to exist and to be the authoritative non-default-cost source (referenced on the rate_limits page; no standalone /api-reference page found at /get-endpoint-costs, which 404s). Response includes default_cost (= 10) for context and lists only routes that deviate from the default. Confirmed non-default costs: Get Order = 2 tokens; Batch Cancel Orders = 2 tokens per order. (Create Order and Batch Create Orders use the default 10.)

endpoints.name: Get Volume Progress (API usage level)
endpoints.purpose: Report trailing-30-day volume and the earn/keep volume goals for each volume-based tier (Premier/Paragon/Prime).
endpoints.key_params: None documented.
endpoints.notes: Path CONFIRMED as referenced in docs (GET /trade-api/v2/account/api_usage_level/volume_progress), but NO dedicated api-reference page was found (candidate URLs 404). Exact response field names/types remain unconfirmed — see open_questions.

endpoints.name: Get Order
endpoints.purpose: Fetch a single order by id; included here because it is a concrete confirmed non-default-cost endpoint.
endpoints.key_params: Path param order_id.
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED: the Get Order reference page states the rate limit is '2 tokens per request' (a non-default cost vs the 10-token default), validating the endpoint_costs example.

endpoints.name: Batch Create Orders
endpoints.purpose: Submit multiple orders in one request; cost is billed per order.
endpoints.key_params: Body field 'orders' = array of CreateOrderRequest. Max batch size 'scales with your tier's write budget' (no fixed number stated in current docs).
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED (V1). Cost = N x 10 tokens (default). The whole batch must fit in the bucket at once or the entire batch is rejected (e.g. 25 orders need 250 tokens available on arrival). 201 returns BatchCreateOrdersResponse { orders: BatchCreateOrdersIndividualResponse[] }, each { client_order_id (nullable), order (Order, nullable), error (ErrorResponse, nullable) }. NOTE: a newer 'Batch Create Orders (V2)' endpoint exists at POST /trade-api/v2/portfolio/events/orders/batched (also body field 'orders', billed N x 10); neither V1 nor V2 publishes a fixed max-order cap.

concepts: Token bucket: each account has Read and Write buckets that refill continuously (no fixed windows, no per-second reset). A request is allowed only if the bucket holds tokens >= the request's cost; otherwise 429.
Budget vs capacity: 'budget' = refill_rate (tokens added per second); 'capacity' = bucket_capacity (max tokens stored). Sustained request rate for an endpoint = budget / cost.
Read vs Write lanes: read (GET/query) operations draw from the Read bucket; order/write operations draw from the Write bucket. They are independent.

Burst: Read buckets and Basic-tier Write buckets hold only 1 second of budget (no burst). Write buckets above Basic (Advanced and above) hold up to 2 seconds, so after ~2 idle seconds you can spend up to 2x the per-second budget in one burst, then throttle to the refill rate.
Five tiers: Basic, Advanced, Premier, Paragon, Prime. Basic on signup; Advanced via self-service upgrade endpoint; Premier/Paragon/Prime earned by volume share or manually assigned.

Volume share = trailing 30-day volume / (previous month's exchange volume x 2). Earn/keep thresholds gate the volume tiers; a qualifying review grants the tier for 30 days (renewed daily while still qualifying; lapses after 30 days if thresholds are no longer met).

Per-endpoint token cost: default 10 tokens; some endpoints cost less (e.g. Get Order = 2, Batch Cancel = 2/order). GET /account/endpoint_costs is the authoritative source of deviations and reports default_cost (= 10).
Batch billing: every item billed separately at the per-item cost; the full batch must fit in the bucket atomically or the entire batch is rejected (all-or-nothing admission).

Two exchange lanes: event_contract (Predictions) and margined (perps). Grants are per exchange_instance, and limits are exposed per lane — GET /account/limits for Predictions and GET /account/limits/perps for the margined/perps lane.
gotchas: Limits are in TOKENS per second, not raw requests per second. Because the default cost is 10 tokens, a Basic read budget of 200 tokens/sec = ~20 requests/sec, and a Basic write budget of 100 = ~10 order ops/sec. Do not equate the budget number with requests/sec.

429 responses currently include NEITHER Retry-After NOR X-RateLimit-* headers. Clients cannot read a server-provided retry delay from headers; they must self-compute backoff (cost / refill_rate gives the minimal wait, e.g. 10 ms at 1,000 tok/s). The docs explicitly say 'Apply exponential backoff on 429.'
No penalty or cooldown on 429 — the bucket keeps refilling, so the next request succeeds as soon as the balance covers the cost. Docs still recommend exponential backoff.
Read buckets do NOT burst (only 1 second of budget). Only Write buckets above Basic tier (Advanced and above) get the 2-second / 2x burst capacity.
Batch admission is atomic: a 25-order create needs all 250 tokens present on arrival; partial admission does not happen — the whole batch is rejected if it doesn't fit.

Self-upgrade only reaches Advanced and requires >=1 of your last 100 Predictions orders to have been API-created; it currently only applies to the Predictions (event_contract) exchange instance. It cannot grant Premier/Paragon/Prime. The upgrade call itself costs 30 tokens against the Predictions Write bucket.

Two separate exchange lanes exist (event_contract / Predictions vs margined / perps); grants are per exchange_instance, and each lane has its own limits endpoint (/account/limits vs /account/limits/perps).
Batch Cancel costs 2 tokens per order while Batch Create costs 10 per order — cancels are cheaper, so cancel-heavy strategies are far less budget-constrained than create-heavy ones.

Two Batch Create endpoints exist: V1 POST /portfolio/orders/batched and V2 POST /portfolio/events/orders/batched. Confirm which your SDK/integration targets; both bill N x 10 and neither publishes a fixed max-order cap.
open_questions: Exact maximum number of orders/items allowed per batch request. RESOLVED-ish: the current Batch Create Orders V1 and V2 pages publish NO fixed integer cap and only say it 'scales with your tier's write budget.' The older 20-order cap appears to be outdated/removed and is not in current docs.

Whether a retry_after_ms field is ever returned by the API on 429. The rate_limits page confirms NO Retry-After/X-RateLimit-* headers and recommends exponential backoff; 'retry_after_ms' appears in third-party client libraries and is likely a client construct, not a server field. Not present in the documented 429 body {"error":"too many requests"}.

Exact response field names/types for GET /trade-api/v2/account/api_usage_level/volume_progress (trailing 30-day volume and earn/keep goals) — endpoint path is referenced in docs but no dedicated api-reference page exists.

Full response schema of GET /account/endpoint_costs (confirmed to include default_cost=10 and a list of deviating routes, but the precise per-route object field names were not published on a standalone reference page).

Whether the margined/perps lane (GET /account/limits/perps) uses the same tier budget numbers as the Predictions lane or a separate schedule — the perps limits endpoint is confirmed to exist but its response schema/numbers were not found on a dedicated reference page.

Exact 403 error message string for the self-upgrade endpoint (docs describe the 403 condition as 'no API-created order in the latest 100 Predictions orders'; the precise 'Criterion not met' wording was not verbatim-confirmed).
verification_notes: Claim 1 (token budgets Basic 200/100, Advanced 300/300, Premier 1000/1000, Paragon 2000/2000, Prime 4000/4000): CONFIRMED verbatim against the tier table on https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/rate_limits.

Claim 2 (default 10 tokens; Get Order = 2; Batch Cancel = 2/order; default_cost=10; authoritative list at GET /account/endpoint_costs): CONFIRMED. Default 10 and Batch Cancel=2/order on the rate_limits page; Get Order='2 tokens per request' on https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/orders/get-order; default_cost field and endpoint_costs role corroborated via docs.kalshi.com search. (rate_limits prose itself only cited Batch Cancel as its example; the Get Order = 2 figure was independently verified on the Get Order page.)

Claim 3 (Read + Basic Write hold 1s; Write above Basic hold up to 2s => up to 2x burst): CONFIRMED verbatim on https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/rate_limits ('Higher-tier Write buckets ... hold up to two seconds of budget ... twice your per-second budget in a single burst').

Claim 4 (429 body {"error":"too many requests"}; no Retry-After / X-RateLimit-*; no penalty/cooldown; exponential backoff): CONFIRMED verbatim on rate_limits page, including the exact guidance 'Apply exponential backoff on 429.'

Claim 5 (POST /account/api_usage_level/upgrade => permanent Advanced grant; requires >=1 of last 100 Predictions orders via API; Predictions/event_contract only; endpoint costs 30 tokens): CONFIRMED against https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/account/upgrade-account-api-usage-level (201 success; 403 if no API-created order in last 100; 429 noting the 30-token cost on the Predictions Write bucket).

Claim 6 (GET /account/limits returns usage_tier, read/write BucketLimit{refill_rate,bucket_capacity}, grants[] ApiUsageLevelGrant{exchange_instance,level,expires_ts,source}): CONFIRMED against https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/account/get-account-api-limits. Minor refinement: expires_ts is a nullable Unix timestamp in SECONDS; exchange_instance is an ExchangeInstance enum (event_contract|margined).

Claim 7 (volume share = trailing-30d / (prev month exchange volume x 2); Premier 0.25%/0.20%, Paragon 0.50%/0.40%, Prime 1.00%/0.80%; grants for 30 days): CONFIRMED verbatim against the volume-tier table and formula on https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/rate_limits (grant renewed daily while qualifying; lapses after 30 days).

Claim 8 (batch billing per-item and atomic: 25 creates=250 tokens, 25 cancels=50 tokens, whole batch must fit or fully rejected): CONFIRMED. Per-item billing and all-or-nothing admission stated on rate_limits page; 10/order (create) and 2/order (cancel) confirmed on the respective order reference pages.

Claim 9 (Batch Create Orders POST /trade-api/v2/portfolio/orders/batched; body 'orders'; max scales with write budget, no fixed integer): CONFIRMED for the V1 endpoint on https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/orders/batch-create-orders. Additive correction: a V2 variant exists at POST /trade-api/v2/portfolio/events/orders/batched (same 'orders' body field, same N x 10 billing); neither version publishes a fixed max-order cap, so the historically cited 20-order cap is not in current docs.

domain: domain-concepts (Kalshi market mechanics & domain model: series/event/market hierarchy, binary resolution & settlement, mutually-exclusive & multivariate collections, fees, collateral, payouts)
summary: Kalshi is a CFTC-regulated, quote-driven (maker/taker) event-contract exchange. Its domain model is a 3-level hierarchy: a Series (recurring template, e.g. KXHIGHNY) contains Events (a real-world occurrence, the basic unit users interact with, e.g. KXHIGHNY-24JAN01), which contain Markets (a single binary YES/NO contract, e.g. KXHIGHNY-24JAN01-T60). Each binary contract pays exactly $1.00 (notional_value_dollars) per contract to the winning side and $0 to the losing side at resolution; YES price + NO price = $1.00 so price = market-implied probability. Most binary markets quote in integer cents 1c-99c, but valid tick sizes are now MARKET-SPECIFIC (read price_level_structure / price_ranges[].step), and since Jan 2026 (Rulebook 13.1) contracts can be traded FRACTIONALLY (down to 1/100 of a contract; fractional_trading_enabled is now always true). An Event has a mutually_exclusive flag (if true, only one of its markets can resolve YES). Trading fees follow a quadratic curve: taker fee = roundup(0.07 x C x P x (1-P)) and the (newer) maker fee = roundup(0.0175 x C x P x (1-P)), where C = number of contracts and P = price in DOLLARS (0-1), with the whole-order total rounded UP to the next cent (taker fees peak at 1.75c/contract at P=0.50). The quadratic maker-fee curve was introduced ~July 2025, replacing a flat $0.0025/share (0.25c) maker fee, and applies only to series whose fee_type is quadratic_with_maker_fees (e.g. major sports and some macro markets); a per-series fee_multiplier scales the coefficient upward for premium markets, and events can override via fee_type_override/fee_multiplier_override. Live per-market fee rates are exposed by GET /trade-api/v2/margin/fee_tiers (maker_fee_rates/taker_fee_rates as decimal fractions). Markets move through a status lifecycle (initialized, inactive, active, closed, determined, disputed, amended, finalized) with settlement_timer_seconds defining the delay between determination and settlement. Collateral for a plain long binary equals price x contracts (the max loss); the optional collateral-return/netting feature (netting_enabled, off by default, locked at your FIRST order in an event) lowers required collateral on mutually-exclusive or hedged positions to the worst-case loss. Multivariate event collections (combos) dynamically build new markets from multiple events and resolve to the PRODUCT of the underlying positions, capped at $1.00 per contract.

endpoints.name: Get Series List
endpoints.purpose: List series (recurring templates) for discovery; top of the hierarchy
endpoints.key_params: category (string), include_product_metadata (bool); filter by tags. Series ticker is the prefix shared by all child events.
endpoints.notes: Returns series objects. The /trade-api/v2 prefix is the standard Kalshi REST base (api.elections.kalshi.com); docs api-reference pages display bare paths (e.g. /series).

endpoints.name: Get Series
endpoints.purpose: Fetch a single series incl. its fee structure and settlement sources
endpoints.key_params: series_ticker (path). Returns ticker, frequency, title, category, tags, settlement_sources[], contract_url, contract_terms_url, fee_type, fee_multiplier, additional_prohibitions[], product_metadata, volume_fp, last_updated_ts
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED against get-series: fee_type enum = quadratic | quadratic_with_maker_fees | flat; fee_multiplier is type number/format double (scales the fee coefficient for that series). settlement_sources[] is an array of SettlementSource objects.

endpoints.name: Get Events
endpoints.purpose: List events (the basic tradeable unit; a collection of markets)
endpoints.key_params: series_ticker, status, with_nested_markets (bool), limit, cursor
endpoints.notes: with_nested_markets=true embeds the markets[] array inside each event; otherwise markets are returned as a separate top-level field.

endpoints.name: Get Event
endpoints.purpose: Fetch one event and its market relationships
endpoints.key_params: event_ticker (path), with_nested_markets (bool). Returns event_ticker, series_ticker, title, sub_title, mutually_exclusive (bool), collateral_return_type, strike_date (nullable date-time), strike_period (nullable), available_on_brokers, category (DEPRECATED), fee_type_override (nullable), fee_multiplier_override (nullable), product_metadata, last_updated_ts, exchange_index
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED against get-event: mutually_exclusive 'If true, only one market in this event can resolve to yes'. category deprecated in favor of series category. NOTE (added): fee_type_override/fee_multiplier_override let an event override the series-level fee config.

endpoints.name: Get Markets
endpoints.purpose: List binary markets with live prices, volume, and settlement metadata
endpoints.key_params: event_ticker, series_ticker, status, tickers, min/max_close_ts, limit, cursor
endpoints.notes: Monetary fields use *_dollars (fixed-point USD string, e.g. '0.0000'); contract counts use *_fp (fixed-point STRING fields, support fractional contracts). Legacy centicent/integer fields were renamed (Jan 2026).

endpoints.name: Get Market
endpoints.purpose: Single market: full lifecycle, strike, and settlement fields
endpoints.key_params: ticker (path). CONFIRMED fields: market_type ('binary'|'scalar'); yes_bid_dollars/yes_ask_dollars/no_bid_dollars/no_ask_dollars; last_price_dollars; previous_yes_bid_dollars/previous_yes_ask_dollars/previous_price_dollars; volume_fp/volume_24h_fp/open_interest_fp; yes_bid_size_fp/yes_ask_size_fp; notional_value_dollars (=$1 settlement value of one contract); settlement_value_dollars (post-determination); result ('yes'|'no'|'scalar'|''); status; strike_type/floor_strike/cap_strike/functional_strike/custom_strike; settlement_timer_seconds (integer); can_close_early; open_time/close_time/expected_expiration_time/latest_expiration_time; fee_waiver_expiration_time; fractional_trading_enabled; price_level_structure/price_ranges; mve_collection_ticker/mve_selected_legs; rules_primary/rules_secondary; is_provisional
endpoints.notes: status enum CONFIRMED: initialized, inactive, active, closed, determined, disputed, amended, finalized. settlement_timer_seconds = seconds after determination until the market settles. CORRECTION: the current schema exposes expected_expiration_time and latest_expiration_time (no plain 'expiration_time' field). price_level_structure + price_ranges[].step give each market's valid tick sizes (ticks are now per-market, not universally 1c). mve_collection_ticker/mve_selected_legs link a market to its combo collection. fractional_trading_enabled is deprecated (Mar 2026) and now always true. liquidity_dollars is a legacy field.

endpoints.name: Get Multivariate Events
endpoints.purpose: Retrieve dynamically-created events produced from multivariate event collections (combos)
endpoints.key_params: collection_ticker (filter), with_nested_markets (bool)
endpoints.notes: Returns EventData objects for the synthetic combo events; collection metadata lives on the collection endpoints. (Path not individually re-fetched in this pass.)

endpoints.name: Get Multivariate Event Collection
endpoints.purpose: Fetch a combo collection definition
endpoints.key_params: collection_ticker (path). Fields: collection_ticker, series_ticker, title, description, open_date, close_date, associated_events[] (AssociatedEvent objs), associated_event_tickers[] (DEPRECATED), is_ordered (bool), is_single_market_per_event (DEPRECATED), is_all_yes (DEPRECATED), size_min/size_max (int32), functional_description
endpoints.notes: size_min/size_max = inclusive min/max number of markets passed into Lookup/Create. is_ordered=true means market order affects output. Prefer associated_events[] over the deprecated flag fields.

endpoints.name: Create Market In Multivariate Event Collection
endpoints.purpose: Materialize a combo market from chosen markets/sides
endpoints.key_params: selected_markets[] = {event_ticker, market_ticker, side ('yes'|'no')}
endpoints.notes: Combo resolves to the PRODUCT of underlying position values, max $1.00/contract; any leg at $0 => whole combo $0. Pricing via RFQ; fills not guaranteed.

endpoints.name: Lookup Tickers For Market In Multivariate Event Collection
endpoints.purpose: Resolve the synthetic event/market ticker for a chosen combination without creating it
endpoints.key_params: selected markets/sides as query input
endpoints.notes: Read-only counterpart to the Create endpoint.

endpoints.name: Get Margin Fee Tiers
endpoints.purpose: Authoritative LIVE source of the actual maker/taker fee rates currently applied
endpoints.key_params: (none / per-market). Returns maker_fee_rates and taker_fee_rates keyed per market ticker, as decimal fractions
endpoints.notes: ADDED endpoint (from changelog): as of May 2026 returns per-market-ticker maker_fee_rates/taker_fee_rates as decimals (previously tier-name maps); June 2026 fix made it return active (non-zero) rates. Use this to confirm the effective 0.07 taker / 0.0175 maker coefficients and any elevated per-series multipliers programmatically.

endpoints.name: Get Portfolio Settlements
endpoints.purpose: History of settled positions incl. realized payout and total fees
endpoints.key_params: limit, cursor, ticker. Returns per-settlement market_result, yes/no count, revenue, settled_time, fees, and (newer) yes_total_cost_dollars/no_total_cost_dollars
endpoints.notes: Authenticated. Confirms $1/contract payout accounting and fee totals at settlement; only net positions are settled.

concepts: Series: a collection of related events sharing a ticker prefix and the same data logic/settlement sources with no outcome dependencies (e.g. KXHIGHNY); a template, not directly traded
Event: a collection of markets and the basic unit members interact with; tied to one series via series_ticker; can be mutually exclusive
Market: a single binary YES/NO contract within an event; a low-level object rarely shown alone
Ticker hierarchy: Series -> Event -> Market (e.g. KXHIGHNY -> KXHIGHNY-24JAN01 -> KXHIGHNY-24JAN01-T60); docs explicitly say 'do not parse ticker strings to infer relationships'
Binary contract: pays $1 (notional_value_dollars) to the winning side, $0 to the loser

Price = probability: YES price + NO price = $1.00 (the combined stake of the matched YES and NO buyers is $1). Most markets quote 1c-99c in 1c ticks, but valid tick sizes are market-specific (price_level_structure / price_ranges[].step), and contracts can be traded fractionally (1/100 contract) since Jan 2026

Maker vs Taker: maker posts a resting limit order (declares side/price/size); taker accepts an existing resting offer and matches immediately. Maker fees are charged only when the resting order executes, not on cancel
mutually_exclusive (Event field): if true only one market in the event resolves YES; enables collateral return
Multivariate event collection / Combo: dynamically builds a new market from multiple events; resolves to the PRODUCT of underlying positions, capped at $1.00/contract (any leg at $0 => combo $0)
Determination vs Settlement: outcome is first 'determined', then after settlement_timer_seconds the market 'settles' and funds transfer; only net positions are settled

Collateral / margin: for a long binary, required collateral = price x contracts = max loss; the optional netting/collateral-return feature reduces it to the worst-case loss for hedged/mutually-exclusive positions

fee_type & fee_multiplier: series-level fields that define whether maker fees apply (quadratic vs quadratic_with_maker_fees vs flat) and scale the fee coefficient; events can override via fee_type_override/fee_multiplier_override
FixedPoint money/count types: *_dollars (fixed-point USD strings) for money; *_fp (fixed-point strings) for contract counts (supports fractional contracts) in the newer API
gotchas: Fee formula P is the price in DOLLARS (0-1), not cents; the curve is symmetric and peaks at P=0.50. Convert cents to dollars before applying.

Rounding is UP (ceiling) on the TOTAL order fee (over all C contracts): fee = ceil_to_cent(coeff x C x P x (1-P)). This makes small orders' effective rate slightly above the headline rate. (Some third-party calculators round per-contract; Kalshi's published schedule rounds the order total up.)

Taker coefficient 0.07 => max 1.75c/contract at 50c. Maker coefficient 0.0175 (one quarter of taker, 'roughly 75% cheaper') => max ~0.44c/contract at 50c. The 0.0025 figure sometimes quoted was the OLD flat $0.0025/share maker fee, replaced by the 0.0175 quadratic curve.

Maker fees only apply to series with fee_type = quadratic_with_maker_fees (the probability-scaled curve rolled out ~July 2025, replacing a flat 0.25c/share fee). They cover mainly major sports and a handful of macro markets; most markets are taker-only.

Ticks are no longer universally 1 cent: read price_level_structure and price_ranges[].step for a market's valid tick sizes. Contracts can also be traded fractionally (down to 1/100 contract) since Jan 2026 (Rulebook 13.1). YES and NO are still two sides of the SAME orderbook (NO price = $1 - YES price).
Each binary contract is collateralized at its purchase price (max loss = premium); you cannot lose more than you paid on a long position.

Collateral return / netting is OFF by default and netting_enabled is locked at the moment of your FIRST order in a given event (even before any fill); it cannot be changed retroactively for that event.
Combos resolve to the PRODUCT of legs (any leg at $0 => whole combo $0), max $1.00; settlement typically occurs within 1-12 hours after the LAST underlying position resolves.
settlement_value_dollars is only populated AFTER determination; notional_value_dollars ($1) is the at-settlement value of one contract.

Deprecated/renamed fields to avoid: event.category (use series category); collection associated_event_tickers/is_single_market_per_event/is_all_yes (use associated_events[]); fractional_trading_enabled (now always true). The current market schema uses expected_expiration_time + latest_expiration_time (not a plain 'expiration_time'); use close_time/latest_expiration_time.
status lifecycle terminal state is 'finalized'; 'determined' = outcome known but not yet paid; settlement happens settlement_timer_seconds after determination.

Newer API monetary fields are suffixed _dollars and returned as fixed-point strings (e.g. '0.0000'); contract counts are suffixed _fp (also fixed-point strings, supporting fractional contracts). Older docs/SDKs used integer-cent/centicent fields (yes_bid, last_price).
open_questions: Exact per-series elevated fee multipliers (fee_multiplier values for premium series like S&P 500, Nasdaq, sports, championship markets): the official kalshi-fee-schedule.pdf is served behind a Vercel security checkpoint (HTTP 429 / JS challenge) and could not be read via WebFetch or curl. The DEFAULT taker (0.07) and maker (0.0175) coefficients are corroborated by multiple 2026 sources but were NOT read from the primary PDF; the live GET /trade-api/v2/margin/fee_tiers endpoint is the authoritative programmatic source.

Exact maker-fee rollout date: third-party sources say the probability-scaled maker curve (0.0175) launched ~July 2025, replacing a flat $0.0025/share fee. The analyst's 'after April 2025' is imprecise; confirm the exact effective date against Kalshi's own schedule/changelog.

Whether the taker/maker total is rounded per-contract or on the whole order: Kalshi's published formula rounds the order total up to the next cent, but some third-party calculators round per-contract; confirm against the primary PDF.
Whether there is an explicitly stated absolute fee cap beyond the natural maximum at P=0.50, and whether settlement fees are always exactly $0 for binary markets.
Precise AssociatedEvent object sub-fields (per-event config such as allowed sides / single-market-per-event) that replace the deprecated collection flag fields were not fully enumerated.
Exact strike_type enum values and how cap_strike/floor_strike/functional_strike map to YES settlement for scalar vs binary markets was only partially captured.
Whether minimum quotable price is still 1c for all markets given per-market price_level_structure/price_ranges[].step (some markets may permit sub-cent or wider ticks).
verification_notes: Taker fee = roundup(0.07 x C x P x (1-P)), P in DOLLARS, total rounded up, max ~1.75c/contract at P=0.50: CONFIRMED on coefficient/curve via GWU paper, marketmath.io, and whirligigbear substack ('0.07 * P * (1-P)', peak 1.75c at 50c). UNVERIFIABLE against the PRIMARY kalshi-fee-schedule.pdf (HTTP 429 / Vercel security checkpoint, also via curl). help.kalshi.com/en/articles/13823805-fees confirms taker fees exist but does not print the formula. Net: coefficient confirmed by strong secondary corroboration; primary PDF blocked.

Maker fee = roundup(0.0175 x C x P x (1-P)), ~one quarter of taker: CONFIRMED coefficient via marketmath.io ('approximately 1.75% x p x (1-p), roughly one quarter of the taker fee') and search corroboration. CORRECTED history/date: the quadratic maker curve launched ~July 2025 (not merely 'after April 2025') and REPLACED a flat $0.0025/share (0.25c) maker fee; applies to fee_type=quadratic_with_maker_fees series (major sports + some macro markets on brokers). Primary PDF UNVERIFIABLE (429).
Series.fee_type enum = quadratic | quadratic_with_maker_fees | flat and fee_multiplier (number/double): CONFIRMED verbatim against https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/market/get-series.

Each binary contract pays $1 to winner / $0 to loser; YES + NO = $1.00: CONFIRMED. https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/market_settlement states 'Yes/No contract holders receive $1 per contract'; https://help.kalshi.com/markets/markets-101/how-are-prices-determined states 'The combined investment of both users must equal $1' and price = probability. CORRECTED: 'prices only in integer cents 1c-99c' is now outdated -- tick sizes are market-specific (price_level_structure/price_ranges[].step) and contracts trade fractionally (1/100) since Jan 2026 (Rulebook 13.1; docs.kalshi.com/changelog).

Hierarchy Series->Event->Market and definitions; do NOT parse tickers: CONFIRMED verbatim against https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/terms ('A series is a collection of related events', 'An event is a collection of markets and the basic unit', 'A single binary market', and 'do not parse ticker strings to infer relationships').

Event.mutually_exclusive (true => only one market resolves YES): CONFIRMED verbatim against https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/events/get-event ('If true, only one market in this event can resolve to yes').

Market status enum (initialized, inactive, active, closed, determined, disputed, amended, finalized) and settlement_timer_seconds (integer, seconds after determination until settlement): CONFIRMED against https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/market/get-market.

Combos resolve to PRODUCT of underlying positions, max $1.00/contract, settle 1-12h after last underlying resolves: CONFIRMED verbatim against https://help.kalshi.com/en/articles/13823820-combos ('The combo payout equals the product of the value of each individual position', 'paying out a maximum of $1.00 per contract', 'within 1 to 12 hours after the last underlying position resolves', 'If any position settles at $0, the entire combo pays $0').

FixedPoint fields: _dollars money strings (yes_bid_dollars, last_price_dollars, notional_value_dollars, settlement_value_dollars) and _fp count fields (volume_fp, open_interest_fp): CONFIRMED against https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/market/get-market and changelog (Jan 2026 added *_fp string contract-count fields; balances became fixed-point dollar strings). _fp are fixed-point STRINGS supporting fractional contracts.

Collateral for long binary = price x contracts (= max loss); netting (netting_enabled) off by default, locked at first order in an event, reduces to worst-case loss for hedged/mutually-exclusive positions: CONFIRMED against https://help.kalshi.com/trading/advanced-options/collateral-return ('disabled by default for new users'; 'netting_enabled is enabled/disabled at the first moment a user places their first order in a given event, even before any trade fills'; 'no way to retroactively enable or disable'; takes only the worst-case loss as collateral).

domain: errors-ops (error handling, rate limits, exchange status/schedule, maintenance, idempotency, communications) — Kalshi Trade API v2
summary: Kalshi's REST API (base https://api.elections.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2 or https://external-api.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2; demo on kalshi.co) returns a generic error envelope on failure with HTTP status codes 400 (bad request), 401 (unauthorized), 409 (conflict/already exists or cannot be modified), 429 (rate limited), and 500/503/504 (server/maintenance). The error body shape is {code, message, details?, service?} (confirmed on the Create Order OpenAPI: code string, message string, details string 'if available', service = name of the service that generated the error), except the rate-limit 429 which the rate-limits page documents as the distinct body {\"error\": \"too many requests\"}. NOTE: docs.kalshi.com/fix/error-handling is FIX-protocol-specific (Reject 35=3, BusinessMessageReject 35=j, ExecutionReport rejects, numeric tags 373/380/103/102) and is NOT the source of the REST envelope; the REST envelope is defined per-endpoint in the OpenAPI. Rate limiting uses a token-bucket model: most requests cost 10 tokens; per-tier read/write budgets range from Basic (read 200 / write 100 tokens/sec) up to Prime (4000/4000); 429s currently carry NO Retry-After or X-RateLimit-* headers and the bucket keeps refilling with no extra penalty/cooldown. Operational endpoints include GET /exchange/status (exchange_active, trading_active, exchange_estimated_resume_time), GET /exchange/schedule (standard_hours per weekday + maintenance_windows, all ET), GET /exchange/announcements (info/warning/error notices), GET /account/limits (live token budgets + grants), and GET /account/endpoint_costs (non-default costs). Scheduled maintenance is every Thursday 3:00–5:00 AM ET as a trading pause (cancels allowed, placements/amends blocked); rare full exchange pauses block cancels too. Idempotency is handled per-domain: orders via client_order_id (though the Create Order reference documents NO explicit idempotency/dedup guarantee), subaccount transfers via client_transfer_id. Communications/milestone data is exposed via GET /milestones and GET /milestones/{milestone_id}. Request signing: KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP in milliseconds; signature concatenation order is timestamp + HTTP_METHOD + path with the query string stripped.

endpoints.name: Get Exchange Status
endpoints.purpose: Detect whether the exchange/trading is live or in a maintenance pause; primary signal for resilient clients to pause order submission
endpoints.key_params: No query params; no auth required (security: [])
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED. Returns ExchangeStatus: exchange_active (bool, REQUIRED — false if the core Kalshi exchange is no longer taking any state changes at all), trading_active (bool, REQUIRED — true if trading currently permitted), exchange_estimated_resume_time (string date-time, OPTIONAL/nullable — estimated downtime end for current maintenance window, not guaranteed and can be extended). 200 success; 500/503/504 return same ExchangeStatus schema.

endpoints.name: Get Exchange Schedule
endpoints.purpose: Retrieve standard trading hours per weekday and planned maintenance windows
endpoints.key_params: No query params; no auth (security: [])
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED. Returns GetExchangeScheduleResponse {schedule:{standard_hours:[WeeklySchedule], maintenance_windows:[{start_datetime,end_datetime}]}}. Each WeeklySchedule has start_time/end_time (date-time, the effective window of that schedule) plus per-weekday arrays monday..sunday of DailySchedule {open_time, close_time}. Per-day open_time/close_time are HH:MM in Eastern Time (ET); outside these times trading is unavailable. Servers: prod external-api.kalshi.com & api.elections.kalshi.com; demo external-api.demo.kalshi.co & demo-api.kalshi.co.

endpoints.name: Get Exchange Announcements
endpoints.purpose: Exchange-wide operational notices (e.g. degradation/maintenance comms)
endpoints.key_params: No auth (security: [])
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED. Returns GetExchangeAnnouncementsResponse {announcements:[{type, message, delivery_time, status}]}. type enum = info|warning|error; status enum = active|inactive; delivery_time is date-time; message is the announcement text.

endpoints.name: Get Account API Limits
endpoints.purpose: Read the authenticated account's live token-bucket budgets and tier grants for rate-limit-aware throttling
endpoints.key_params: Auth required (KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY/-SIGNATURE/-TIMESTAMP; timestamp in ms; RSA-PSS base64 signature)
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED. Returns GetAccountApiLimitsResponse {usage_tier (string), read:BucketLimit, write:BucketLimit, grants:[ApiUsageLevelGrant]}. BucketLimit = {refill_rate (int, tokens added per second), bucket_capacity (int, max tokens)}. Grant = {exchange_instance: event_contract|margined, level (string e.g. premier/paragon/prime), source: volume|manual, expires_ts (int64 unix SECONDS, nullable/absent for permanent grants)}. grants array added June 11 2026; a parallel /account/limits/perps exists.

endpoints.name: Get Account Endpoint Costs
endpoints.purpose: Authoritative list of routes whose token cost differs from the default 10
endpoints.key_params: Public endpoint, no auth (security: []); added per changelog 2026-04-22
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED path /trade-api/v2/account/endpoint_costs (reference page slug is 'list-non-default-endpoint-costs'). Response: {default_cost (int, =10 for unlisted routes), endpoint_costs:[{method, path, cost}]}. Use to discover non-default per-request token costs currently in effect; this table is dynamic.

endpoints.name: Get Milestones
endpoints.purpose: Communication/event-milestone data (sports games, elections, etc.) for tracking outcomes
endpoints.key_params: limit (int 1-500, REQUIRED), minimum_start_date (RFC3339 date-time), category (e.g. Sports|Elections|Esports|Crypto), competition (e.g. Pro Football/Pro Basketball (M)/...), source_id, type (e.g. football_game/basketball_game/political_race), related_event_ticker, cursor, min_updated_ts (int64 Unix SECONDS — poll for changes). No auth.
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED. Milestone object: id (string), category (string), type (string), start_date (date-time), end_date (date-time nullable), title, notification_message, related_event_tickers (array), primary_event_tickers (array), last_updated_ts (string date-time), source_id (string nullable), source_ids (object), details (object). Paginated via cursor.

endpoints.name: Get Milestone
endpoints.purpose: Fetch a single milestone by id
endpoints.key_params: Path param milestone_id; no auth (security: [])
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED. Same Milestone object as Get Milestones.

endpoints.name: Create Order (error/idempotency reference)
endpoints.purpose: Canonical example of the REST error envelope and order idempotency via client_order_id
endpoints.key_params: Body: ticker (min len 1), side(yes|no), action(buy|sell) required; client_order_id optional; count optional; yes_price/no_price 1-99; expiration_ts is Unix SECONDS (int64); time_in_force, post_only, reduce_only, order_group_id, etc.
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED path POST /portfolio/orders; success = 201 (Order created). Documented error responses: 400 (BadRequestError invalid input), 401 (UnauthorizedError), 409 (ConflictError already exists or cannot be modified), 429 (RateLimitError), 500 (InternalServerError). All use the generic error schema {code, message, details?, service?}. No idempotency/dedup guarantee for client_order_id is documented on this page.

concepts: Token-bucket rate limiting: each request deducts tokens (default 10); bucket refills at refill_rate tokens/sec up to bucket_capacity; request allowed only if bucket holds enough tokens, else HTTP 429 — no extra penalty/cooldown, the bucket keeps refilling normally
Separate read vs write buckets: GETs hit the read bucket; order place/amend/cancel, order groups, RFQ flows, and block-trade accepts hit the write bucket

Usage tiers earned by trading volume: Basic, Advanced, Premier, Paragon, Prime (Premier/Paragon/Prime are now earned automatically from trailing trading volume, can also be granted manually, and are each backed by a grant viewable in /account/limits grants array)
Trading pause vs exchange pause: trading pause blocks placements/amends but allows cancels; exchange pause blocks cancels too (resting orders remain on the book in both)
Generic REST error envelope {code, message, details?, service?} vs the rate-limit-specific 429 body {"error":"too many requests"}
Market lifecycle status field drives whether orders are accepted (initialized/active/inactive/closed/determined/disputed/amended/finalized)
Idempotency keys: client_order_id (orders), client_transfer_id (subaccount transfers) — note the Create Order reference does not document a hard dedup guarantee for client_order_id
Exchange status polling as the resilience signal: clients should check trading_active/exchange_active before submitting
Eastern Time (ET) is the reference timezone for schedule and maintenance windows

Request signing: KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP in milliseconds; signature is computed over the concatenation timestamp + HTTP_METHOD + path (e.g. '1703123456789GET/trade-api/v2/portfolio/balance'), with the query string stripped (path.split('?')[0]); signature is base64 RSA-PSS over the SHA-256 of that string
gotchas: 429 responses do NOT currently include a Retry-After header or X-RateLimit-* headers — clients must implement their own backoff (exponential + jitter); no cooldown is applied, the bucket just refills

Two different 429 bodies appear in docs: the rate_limits page states the literal body {"error":"too many requests"}, while the per-endpoint OpenAPI lists 429 RateLimitError under the generic {code,message,details?,service?} schema — verify which actually ships in production

The signing concatenation order is timestamp + HTTP_METHOD + path (NOT method+timestamp+path). KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP must be in MILLISECONDS, not seconds — a seconds timestamp produces a signature/auth error
When signing requests, strip query params from the path (sign path.split('?')[0]) or the signature fails

expiration_ts is in Unix SECONDS; orders with expiration_ts in the past are rejected with 'Expiration timestamp must be in the future' (since 2025-11-21); cannot set both time_in_force immediate_or_cancel and expiration_ts
Batch orders are billed per item: N orders = N×10 tokens (or N×per-order cost) and the ENTIRE batch must fit in the write bucket at once
Legacy /portfolio/orders mutation and batch endpoints cost 10x the corresponding V2 /portfolio/events/orders endpoints (POST ≈50–100, DELETE ≈10–20 tokens) since 2026-06-04 — budget accordingly
Maintenance is every Thursday 3:00–5:00 AM ET; expect WebSocket/session disconnects and reconnect only after 5:00 AM ET
exchange_estimated_resume_time is optional and explicitly NOT guaranteed — windows can be extended; don't hard-depend on it
Market status filter values differ from raw status: filter 'open'→active, 'paused'→inactive, 'unopened'→initialized, 'settled'→finalized
Perps/margined traffic uses independent rate-limit buckets separate from event-contract buckets
'Pending' order status was removed from the status enum (announced 2025-11-20, released ~2025-11-27); GET /events excludes multivariate events since 2025-12-04 (use /events/multivariate)

WebSocket error code 25 = 'Subscription buffer overflow' (added 2026-05-12); orderbook sessions enforce max 500k market subscriptions and 10k commands/sec (announced effective 2026-06-18) and error on unknown tickers on the orderbook_delta channel

The docs.kalshi.com/fix/error-handling page is FIX-protocol-only (Reject/BusinessMessageReject/ExecutionReport, tags 373/380/103/102) — do NOT treat it as the REST error-code reference; the REST envelope lives in the per-endpoint OpenAPI
open_questions: Exact full list of REST error 'code' string values and their HTTP status mappings is not published on a single page — only fragments (the 2026-01-26 order-validation codes) are confirmed; the complete enum needs the full OpenAPI spec or a dedicated error-codes page. The fix/error-handling page is FIX-only and does not cover REST codes
Whether the 429 body in production is the generic {code,message,...} envelope (per-endpoint OpenAPI) or the literal {"error":"too many requests"} (rate_limits page) — the two docs disagree
Whether the 'service' field of the REST error envelope is always present or optional (details is qualified 'if available'; service has no such qualifier on the Create Order page)
Whether client_order_id provides a hard idempotency/dedup guarantee on Create Order and Batch Create Orders, and over what time window — explicitly NOT documented on the endpoint reference page

Whether placing an order during a trading pause returns a specific HTTP status (e.g. 409 or 400) and a specific error code (e.g. 'market_inactive' / 'exchange_closed') — the maintenance page does not specify the REST error response
Exact per-route token costs (the live contents of GET /account/endpoint_costs) — only that default_cost is 10 and legacy order mutations are 10x V2; the full cost table is dynamic
The full WebSocket public error-code list (added 2026-05-12) beyond code 25 (subscription buffer overflow) was not individually enumerated here

Whether 503/504 are formally returned during exchange maintenance for REST calls, or whether clients must instead poll /exchange/status — docs only state the status pages return the ExchangeStatus schema for 500/503/504
verification_notes: CLAIM 1 (REST error envelope {code,message,details?,service?}): CONFIRMED via Create Order OpenAPI — code (string 'Error code'), message (string), details (string 'Additional details about the error, if available'), service (string 'name of the service that generated the error'). CORRECTED SOURCING: the cited docs.kalshi.com/fix/error-handling page is FIX-protocol-only (Reject 35=3, BusinessMessageReject 35=j, ExecutionReport, tags 373/380/103/102) and is NOT the REST envelope source. https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/orders/create-order

CLAIM 2 (429 body + no headers): CONFIRMED — rate_limits page gives the exact body {"error": "too many requests"} and states '429 responses do not currently include Retry-After or X-RateLimit-* headers'; no penalty/cooldown, bucket refills normally. https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/rate_limits

CLAIM 3 (default cost 10; tier budgets): CONFIRMED exactly — default 10 tokens; Basic 200/100, Advanced 300/300, Premier 1000/1000, Paragon 2000/2000, Prime 4000/4000 (read/write tokens per second). https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/rate_limits

CLAIM 4 (Exchange Status fields): CONFIRMED — exchange_active (bool, required), trading_active (bool, required), exchange_estimated_resume_time (string date-time, optional/nullable). Spelling and optionality exactly as claimed. https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/exchange/get-exchange-status

CLAIM 5 (Thursday 3-5 AM ET maintenance; trading vs exchange pause): CONFIRMED — 'Every Thursday from 3:00 AM to 5:00 AM ET'; trading pause: cancel orders YES, place/amend NO; exchange pause: cancel orders NO, place/amend NO; orders remain on book. https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/maintenance_and_pauses

CLAIM 6 (timestamp ms; sign path without query): CONFIRMED that KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP is in milliseconds and the query string is stripped (path.split('?')[0]). CORRECTED: the concatenation order is timestamp + HTTP_METHOD + path (docs example '1703123456789GET/trade-api/v2/portfolio/balance'), NOT 'method+timestamp+path' as the original concept stated — fixed in concepts/gotchas. https://docs.kalshi.com/getting_started/quick_start_authenticated_requests

CLAIM 7 (order-validation codes added 2026-01-26): CONFIRMED — Jan 26 2026 changelog adds invalid_order_size, available_balance_too_low, order_id_and_client_order_id_mismatch, order_side_mismatch, order_ticker_mismatch, replacing the generic invalid_order in certain cases. https://docs.kalshi.com/changelog

CLAIM 8 (client_order_id idempotency not documented on Create Order): CONFIRMED — the Create Order reference documents no idempotency/deduplication behavior for client_order_id. https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/orders/create-order

CLAIM 9 (grants[] item fields): CONFIRMED — ApiUsageLevelGrant = exchange_instance (ExchangeInstance enum event_contract|margined), level (string, e.g. premier/paragon/prime), source (volume|manual), expires_ts (int64 Unix seconds, nullable/absent for permanent). Spellings exact. https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/account/get-account-api-limits

CLAIM 10 (Exchange Schedule structure): CONFIRMED — standard_hours is an array of WeeklySchedule {start_time, end_time (date-time effective window), monday..sunday arrays of DailySchedule {open_time, close_time}} with open_time/close_time as HH:MM in ET; maintenance_windows is an array of {start_datetime, end_datetime}. https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/exchange/get-exchange-schedule

EXTRA — endpoint_costs path: CONFIRMED GET /trade-api/v2/account/endpoint_costs (public, security: []); response {default_cost, endpoint_costs:[{method, path, cost}]}; added 2026-04-22. https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/account/list-non-default-endpoint-costs

EXTRA — Create Order path/success: CONFIRMED POST /portfolio/orders; success status 201. expiration_ts is 'Unix timestamp in seconds'. side enum yes|no, action enum buy|sell. https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/orders/create-order

EXTRA — changelog dates: CONFIRMED legacy /portfolio/orders 10x V2 /portfolio/events/orders (2026-06-04), grants array (2026-06-11), endpoint_costs public (2026-04-22), past expiration_ts rejected (2025-11-21), pending status removed (announced 2025-11-20), GET /events excludes multivariate (2025-12-04), WS code 25 buffer overflow (2026-05-12), orderbook 500k subs / 10k cmds-per-sec (effective 2026-06-18, an announced-upcoming change relative to today 2026-06-13). https://docs.kalshi.com/changelog

EXTRA — Milestones: CONFIRMED GET /milestones (limit required 1-500, no auth) and GET /milestones/{milestone_id} (no auth); min_updated_ts is int64 Unix seconds while the object's last_updated_ts is a date-time string. https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/milestone/get-milestones

RSA-PSS SHA256 signs timestamp then method then path, timestamp in MILLISECONDS; headers KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY, KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP, KALSHI-ACCESS-SIGNATURE; SDK sets Configuration.api_key_id and private_key_pem
